races.race_name: bet365 Handicap

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut at Ripon (6f, good) last August and won Kempton novice (6f, AW) in November on fourth start; some good AW handicap efforts since but not last time and he's probably vulnerable against unexposed types.
runners.comment: Some good AW handicap form but he's probably vulnerable against unexposed types

runners.name: Old Is Gold
runners.spotlight: Midfield in big-field nursery at York's Ebor meeting (6f, good to firm) last August when last seen; gelded since; however, close third in a nursery at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good to soft) previously, showing good pace, and he's in the picture on that form; also effective on good/good to firm.
runners.comment: Midfield at York last August but close third in nursery at Glorious Goodwood previously

runners.name: Atticum
runners.spotlight: Built upon last September's debut (5f, AW) to go close at Nottingham (5f, good to soft), then went one better at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) in October when last seen; he could do with breaking more professionally but he impressed last time and could have plenty more to offer; yard in good form.
runners.comment: Impressed at Yarmouth when last seen & could have plenty more to offer; yard in good form

runners.name: Ambishio
runners.spotlight: 0-5 last year, all on turf, but he showed promise and won a handicap at Southwell (5f, AW) in January on his first run since being gelded; close second at Southwell (5f, AW) a fortnight ago and could be thereabouts if the return to grass isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW this year, having been gelded; thereabouts if return to turf is no issue

runners.name: Calico Blue
runners.spotlight: Won at Chester (6f, good) last June on second start and comfortably followed up in novice there (6f, good) the following month; absent since and this handicap debut is a much tougher test of his credentials, but he's a bright prospect and it remains to be seen where his limitations lie.
runners.comment: Absent since last July but twice looked good at Chester last summer; handicap debut

runners.name: Sir Alfie
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer for Ger Lyons but down the field in Listed races last autumn on both starts for Charles Byrnes and soundly beaten in a handicap at Newmarket last Thursday on his stable/seasonal debut; others preferred.
runners.comment: Below par on final two starts last term and same story on recent reappearance at Newmarket

runners.name: Hypnotised
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of his first two starts (6f, good/AW) when readily winning a Southwell maiden (5f, AW) at odds-on last September; his attractive pedigree provides optimism that he can take higher order still.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden last September; attractive pedigree and could take higher order still

runners.name: Underwriter
runners.spotlight: Won maiden/novice races at Ayr (6f, good to firm/good) last season on two of his first three starts; came up short in Group/sales races otherwise but reappears having been gelded and he could prove well suited by this first crack at 5f; interesting on handicap debut, especially if backed.
runners.comment: 6f maiden/novice wins last year; returns having been gelded; could be suited by drop to 5f

runners.name: Black Star Boy
runners.spotlight: Won with something to spare at Newbury (6f, good to soft) last September on second start then pulled hard when mid-division in Listed race at York (6f, good); gelded since, which may settle him down, and this well-bred sort has potential off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Won in good style at Newbury last September; well bred and has potential off opening mark

races.race_name: bet365 Esher Cup Handicap

runners.name: Laureate Crown
runners.spotlight: Didn't really kick on last year after winning at Ascot (7f, good to firm) on debut but he reappeared at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month having been gelded and defied a slow start to win by half a length; it was a small field but the clear runner-up finished second in a good handicap at Newmarket subsequently and this fellow may well be in the mix up 6lb.
runners.comment: Reappearance win came in small field but the form has been franked by the clear runner-up

runners.name: Look To The Stars
runners.spotlight: Won Kempton novice (7f, AW) last September on third start and followed up in warm conditions race at Newbury (1m, good to soft); failed to settle and didn't stay 1m2f in Group 3 Zetland Stakes at Newmarket last seen; wind op since and the hood could be a positive on reappearance; chance.
runners.comment: Two 2yo wins; failed to stay 1m2f when last seen; the hood could be a positive on return

runners.name: Nobody Knows
runners.spotlight: Well-backed winner at Ayr (7f, soft) last September on sole 2yo start and made it 2-2 in Wolverhampton novice (8.6f, AW) last month, again doing it cosily; has potential off his opening mark and the booking of Ryan Moore is a bonus.
runners.comment: 2-2, cosily as favourite each time; Ryan Moore booked; has potential off opening mark

runners.name: Organise
runners.spotlight: Impressive on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) last November and made it 2-2 in Yarmouth novice (1m, good) on recent reappearance; his inexperience was still evident last time and this well-bred colt could have lots more left in the tank for last year's winning yard; key player on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2-2 and could have lots more left in the tank for last year's winning yard; key player

runners.name: Capall Rasa
runners.spotlight: Improved upon first two runs (both on turf) to win at Southwell (7f, AW) last October, and followed up on handicap debut there (1m, AW); had just three rivals to beat last time and he's 12lb higher today with something to prove on turf, but is not discounted given the ease of that last win.
runners.comment: Best 2yo form came on AW but did it easily last time and he's not discounted back on turf

runners.name: Suspended Sentence
runners.spotlight: Won Ripon novice (1m, good to firm) last August on third start then followed up in four-runner nursery over this C&D (soft); creditable Listed fourth in France (1m, soft) in September but returns to a handicap off a mark 13lb higher than for his course win; further improvement needed.
runners.comment: Won small-field nursery over C&D last September but makes reappearance off 13lb higher
runners.owner: G C Myddelton, C F E Hill & Partner

runners.name: Goodwood Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) last June and kept on for fourth in Group 2 Vintage Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) next time; ran okay in quality conditions race at Newbury (1m, good to soft) in September when last seen but he needs to prove he can cut it off this lofty mark.
runners.comment: Good fourth in Group 2 at Goodwood last July but has to prove he can cut it off this mark

races.race_name: bet365 Gordon Richards Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Almeric
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-5; has gained all wins, including two Listed races, in his starts on softer than good but may yet show that he's fully effective on faster ground; drops back in class having been comfortably held in the Champion Stakes; top on the figures and he was progressive before that latest start.
runners.comment: Record is 3-5; progressive before latest start (Champion Stakes); top on the figures

runners.name: Saddadd
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in Listed race at Goodwood on last appearance but was far from disgraced, especially as he could have done with a stronger pace; progressive otherwise and landed a notable handicap at Newbury (good to firm) prior to following up over C&D (soft); remains of major interest.
runners.comment: Progressive until last outing when not disgraced; C&D winner; remains of major interest

runners.name: Devil's Advocate
runners.spotlight: Highlight last season was a very useful performance in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster, suggesting he's verging on Group class; creditable second, despite usual cheekpieces being removed, in AW Listed event at Kempton on reappearance; possibilities with the headgear back on.
runners.comment: Verging on Group class and has possibilities with usual cheekpieces reapplied

runners.name: King Of Cities
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Richard Hannon but remained with same owner; produced his best performance in most recent domestic start, namely a narrow success in Group 3 at last year's Ebor festival at York (1m1f, good to firm); may have more to offer this term; respected.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner for Richard Hannon in most recent domestic start; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Muddy Mooy
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW and she did well to finish third, in difficult circumstances, at Kempton (1m2f Listed) on reappearance; ties in with Devil's Advocate on that bare form but isn't guaranteed to find the required further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Ran well at Kempton on reappearance but isn't sure to progress further back on turf

races.race_name: bet365 Mile (Group 2)

runners.name: Opera Ballo
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who readily bagged Group 1 honours at Meydan (1m1f, good) three months ago, taking record to 6-8; however, appears to need further improvement to defy 5lb penalty and was no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic outing.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at Meydan last time; no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic start

runners.name: Field Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Very progressive until having plausible excuses in the Sussex Stakes (found to be lame) and QEII (draw to some extent) in last two appearances; impressive wins in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and St James's Palace Stakes make him the one to beat, provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Impressive in Irish 2,000 Guineas and SJP prior to excusable defeats; sets the standard

runners.name: Never So Brave
runners.spotlight: Did very well (3-5) last season for new stable, completing a notable summer hat-trick (7f/1m, good to firm) that culminated in Group 1 success at York; not disgraced from unfavourable draw in the QEII on final outing; may rate higher still and looks likely to go well.
runners.comment: Did very well last season for new yard; not disgraced in QEII; may rate higher still

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive in his first campaign last year and still unbeaten (4-4); won going away from Opera Ballo in Group 2 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on final start; remains one to follow and has the makings of a Group 1 colt this term; commands respect.
runners.comment: Highly progressive in his first season and still unbeaten (4-4); remains one to follow
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who finished third in this race last year; returns to Sandown but may be flattered by his new rating, having been perhaps helped by the draw when registering a 100-1 success in the Group 1 QEII at Ascot (good) on last appearance; needs to prove he can back up that form.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up the form of his 100-1 win in the Group 1 QEII

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Below best in three of his last four outings in 2025 but commenced the campaign splendidly, winning twice (including in this race) then going close in the Group 1 Lockinge; while today may be a good time to catch him, he faces a slightly stiffer assignment in this field.
runners.comment: Won this race last year but is facing a slightly stiffer assignment in this field

races.race_name: bet365 Classic Trial (Group 3)

runners.name: Action
runners.spotlight: Closely related to last year's Derby winner Lambourn and has his own Epsom aspirations, having ended a progressive 2yo campaign with close second to one of his stablemates in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy); now goes in tongue-tie; the pick on the figures and may well rate higher still.
runners.comment: Ended progressive 2yo campaign with close second in the Group 1 Futurity; the form pick
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Al Zanati
runners.spotlight: Dubawi colt who got up in the final strides to win 1m1f AW Group 3 at Chantilly on final 2yo start, taking record to 2-3; looks likely to be well suited by going up further in distance; doesn't have a Derby entry but further progress may materialise.
runners.comment: AW Group 3 win in France took his record to 2-3; further progress may materialise

runners.name: Winding Stream
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, scoring comfortably at Southwell (7f) as a 2yo then running well under a penalty at Kempton (1m); holds a Derby entry and may be suited by going up further in distance today but faces a stiff task at this level; bottom on bare figures.
runners.comment: AW form comprises a win and good second; faces a stiffer task at this level

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching second in the Convivial Maiden at York then went one better at Yarmouth and ran well in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) upped further in trip; developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer.
runners.comment: Developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer

runners.name: Oceans Four
runners.spotlight: Narrowly bagged Group 3 honours at Chantilly (1m, soft) in September when last seen but still needs to prove he can repeat the clear peak form he appeared to show when going close at 25-1 in an exciting Solario at Sandown; gelding who has no major entries in the future.
runners.comment: Clear peak form when running well in the Solario; won French Group 3 next time

runners.name: Wise Prince
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt whose two major entries include the Derby; scored comfortably at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in sole 2yo start, looking the type who'll be suited by middle distances and could develop into a Group performer this year; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Nottingham, looking the type who could become a Group colt

runners.name: Raaheeb
runners.spotlight: Smartly bred, being a brother to Baaeed and Hukum; duly showed strong promise in justifying favouritism at Ascot (7f, soft) in sole 2yo outing; held a Royal Lodge entry at the time and currently holds three major entries, including the Derby; should be suited by middle distances; bright prospect.
runners.comment: Smartly bred; justified favouritism at Ascot in sole 2yo outing; bright prospect

races.race_name: bet365 "Wild Card" Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ribbon Of Sea
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) on reappearance and is the type to rate higher still, being a Sea The Stars filly who cost 1,600,000gns as a yearling; respected.
runners.comment: Scored at Wolverhampton on reappearance and is the type to rate higher still

runners.name: Bintsaleh
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly who cost 925,000gns as a yearling and holds an Oaks entry; both starts at Yarmouth, better effort when third upped to 1m2f latest; possibilities with another step forward likely.
runners.comment: Fared better upped to 1m2f in latest Yarmouth start; Oaks entry; possibilities

runners.name: Sunshine Star
runners.spotlight: Entered in the Oaks, which her half-sister Soul Sister won for her connections in 2023; close second and clear of remainder at Kempton (1m, AW) in sole 2yo run, faring best of the newcomers; a winner waiting to happen and her yard has a good record in this race.
runners.comment: Oaks entry; close second at Kempton in sole 2yo run; winner waiting to happen

runners.name: Palomezcal
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; Space Blues half-sister to five winners, notably California Dreamer (1m AW; Irish 1,000 Guineas runner-up; RPR 110); dam German Oaks winner; check the betting.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; by Space Blues out of German Oaks winner

runners.name: Alma Latina
runners.spotlight: Debutante by Study Of Man out of a 1m2f winner (including Listed; RPR 105) for her connections; interesting, especially if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Debutante by Study Of Man out of Listed winner for her connections; interesting

runners.name: Sullom Voe
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros yearling; New Bay half-sister to five winners, notably Patrick Sarsfield (1m-1m2f including Group 3; RPR 114); one of two newcomers for her stable; market instructive.
runners.comment: 110,000euros yearling; by New Bay; one of two newcomers for her stable

runners.name: Ruby Wedding
runners.spotlight: Something to find on her October efforts (1m) but remains open to improvement, being an Oaks entry who is related to several winners for her owner-breeder.
runners.comment: Something to find on her October efforts but remains open to improvement

runners.name: Felicitas
runners.spotlight: Oaks entry who won well at Lingfield (1m, AW) in sole 2yo start and looks open to progress this term; faces fairly deep opposition but defying a 6lb penalty is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Oaks entry who won well at Lingfield (AW) in sole 2yo start; open to progress

runners.name: Ladykirk
runners.spotlight: Oaks entry who showed plenty of promise at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) six months ago, beating all bar the market leader and faring best of the newcomers; enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: Oaks entry; plenty of promise at Yarmouth, beating all bar the market leader

runners.name: Corallience
runners.spotlight: May do better in due course but is perhaps one for handicaps later on; bottom of this pack judged on her 2yo effort at Newbury (1m, soft).
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack judged on her 2yo effort at Newbury

races.race_name: Nordoff & Robbins Ozzy Osbourne Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Into The Light
runners.spotlight: Defied inexperience to win on debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February and again ran well when 3l second to a newcomer at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last month; his dam was a Listed winner and he could prove to be on an advantageous mark for his first handicap start.
runners.comment: Godolphin colt; could prove to be on an advantageous mark for his first handicap start

runners.name: Magic Trip
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (1m, good to soft) last September before 8l last of eight in Italian Group 3 (7.5f, good); reappeared with strong finish for third in Kempton novice (1m, AW) this month and his pedigree also suggests he'll relish the extra yardage today; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Highly likely that he'll relish going beyond 1m for the first time; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Colourband
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to exciting prospect at Kempton (1m, AW) in December on third and final 2yo start and returned to action with 4l win in Bath novice (1m2f, good) this month; likeable prospect who is respected on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Returned to action with 4l win in recent Bath novice and he's respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Bnaider
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when up in trip and third at Newbury (1m, soft) last October on his third start; ran well on good ground on debut; there's stamina in his pedigree and he's a possible improver on this first handicap start.
runners.comment: There's stamina in his pedigree; possible improver up in trip on this first handicap start

runners.name: The Joker
runners.spotlight: Best of his four 2yo runs came when second here (7f, soft) in August, and he also showed promise on fast ground; bred to be suited by the step up in trip but he was headstrong on occasions last year, which is a concern, and improvement is needed off his opening mark.
runners.comment: 0-4 last year; bred to be suited by the longer trip but needs improvement off opening mark

runners.name: Lost Boys
runners.spotlight: Promising third last year on first two starts (1m, good/soft) then won Haydock maiden (1m, good to soft) in September; his dam was a 1m2f/1m4f winner and it seems likely that he'll improve for this step up in trip on handicap/seasonal debut; trainer won this in 2024.
runners.comment: Promising over 1m on his three 2yo starts and bred to appreciate this longer trip

races.race_name: GS Group Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Misterdoc
runners.spotlight: Ran on well to win at Ludlow two months ago (soft) and three of the next four home have won since; sets the standard here by some way.
runners.comment: The Ludlow win has worked out well and he sets the standard here by some way

runners.name: Sears Crossing
runners.spotlight: No wins in six on the Flat for Jessica Harrington but showed a fair level of ability at up to 1m4f and joined this yard for 40,000gns; lacks jumping experience but raw ability is respected.
runners.comment: Useful Flat maiden in Ireland and joined this strong yard for 40,000gns

runners.name: Eustace Grenier
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in all three bumpers (soft/good) but Sean Bowen rides one of his stablemates.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise in all three bumpers but rejected by stable jockey

runners.name: Mr Maximillian
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous from off the pace when sixth in a Wetherby bumper (good to soft; 15-2); one of three runners for the yard.
runners.comment: Never dangerous from off the pace when sixth in a Wetherby bumper (15-2)

runners.name: Palavar
runners.spotlight: Minor Flat form in six Irish starts for two different trainers; wouldn't be the obvious solution.
runners.comment: Minor Flat form in six Irish starts for two different trainers; opposable

runners.name: C'Mon So
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish point but it's been modest stuff under rules; readily opposed.
runners.comment: Runner-up in an Irish point but it's been modest stuff under rules; readily opposed

runners.name: Brewdog
runners.spotlight: 125-1 when tailed off in a heavy-ground bumper at Ayr in January.
runners.comment: 125-1 when tailed off in a heavy-ground bumper at Ayr in January

runners.name: Jack Dempsey
runners.spotlight: Unseated quite early in his first two runs over hurdles but finished a remote ninth at Ayr when 150-1; the only positive is the return to a drier surface, as the ground was good when runner-up on bumper debut.
runners.comment: Couldn't recommend on hurdle form but ran his best race in a good-ground bumper

races.race_name: QuinnBet Highland National Handicap Chase (For The Julian Llewellen Palmer Memorial Trophy) (GBB)

runners.name: Busty Boy
runners.spotlight: Only 1-21 overall and clearly hard to win with, but he was runner-up in this 12 months ago and returns in form having been running well from 2m4f to 3m; in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this 12 months ago and returns in form; in the mix off a competitive mark

runners.name: Upfordebate
runners.spotlight: Went chasing last summer and he's won four times from 3m to 3m2f; now high in the ratings but still ran very well from the front at Carlisle last month (3m); unproven over this far and he does race up with the pace.
runners.comment: Four wins this season; unproven over this far and he does race up with the pace

runners.name: President Scottie
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner; has been expensive to follow over fences (0-9) and fair to say that others arrive here in better form.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow over fences and fair to say that others arrive here in better form

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: Not as good as he was but has turned a corner following wind surgery and came home clear at Sedgefield (3m2f, good); that race fell apart in behind but he would probably have won anyway; raised 8lb but had become very well handicapped.
runners.comment: Raised 8lb for his Sedgefield romp but had become very well handicapped

runners.name: Express Surprise
runners.spotlight: Only defeat in his last four races came in a good-quality 3m4f handicap at Windsor; jumped well and stayed on best at Chepstow last time (2m7f, soft) where the front two pulled well clear; raised 7lb but Ned Fox takes away 3lb out of that; respected.
runners.comment: Sound jumper who stays well and he's won three of his last four; progressive

runners.name: Fever Dream
runners.spotlight: If the ground is bordering on good then this 8yo could take some beating as he hosed up at Warwick last month (3m, good) on his second run after a break; the 8lb rise isn't the end of the world.
runners.comment: Hosed up at Warwick and could take some beating if the ground is bordering on good
runners.owner: Bedford & Noel & The Can't Say No

runners.name: Breeze Of Wind
runners.spotlight: Pulled up two runs ago but solid efforts in defeat on either side of that and he's an assured stayer at this trip; the blinkers are new, as were the cheekpieces when third at Kelso last month.
runners.comment: Pulled up two runs back but solid efforts in defeat on either side of that; assured stayer

runners.name: El Granjero
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who is 0-5 over fences but three times runner-up in this discipline and latest Warwick conqueror (3m5f, good to soft) was completing a hat-trick; likely to run well off an untouched mark.
runners.comment: Latest Warwick conqueror (3m5f, good to soft) was completing a hat-trick

runners.name: Fortunedefortunata
runners.spotlight: Had smart form when with Gordon Elliott before losing his way and selling for £3,000; fell midrace on stable debut but, with the cheekpieces back on, he outran odds of 100-1 to finish a fairly close third at Haydock (3m4f, good); if as good again here then he could have some say.
runners.comment: 100-1 third at Haydock and if as good again here he could have some say

runners.name: Brandt
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m4f handicap chase at Leopardstown in March of last year (yielding); this season hasn't gone so well but it wasn't too bad a run on his return to fences at Downpatrick last time (3m4f; yielding to soft) and unwise to put a line through him.
runners.comment: 3m4f chase winner last year and some sign of returning to form on his last run

runners.name: Noble Birth
runners.spotlight: 3-24 over hurdles and 1-8 as a chaser; left Gavin Cromwell after finishing tailed off in a claiming hurdle in early March (tried blinkers); needs a revival and he's got a lot of weight.
runners.comment: Last seen tailed off in an Irish claiming hurdle; needs a revival and has topweight

races.race_name: Perth Racecourse Racing & Events Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Ran no race over hurdles on final run for previous yard (tried a visor) and again looked a tricky ride back over fences on debut for this one; the return of cheekpieces may help but she's very risky at the minute.
runners.comment: Beginning to look a tricky ride and she's very risky at the minute

runners.name: Roaring Conquest
runners.spotlight: A winner the last time he contested a Class 3 handicap (on reappearance) and he was boxing on when beaten a little under 10l in a deep Uttoxeter handicap last time out (2m7f, soft); looks sure to stay this far and has the assistance of Sean Bowen.
runners.comment: Positive last run; looks sure to stay this far and has the assistance of Sean Bowen

runners.name: Inferno Sacree
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; having his 40th career start but a first over 3m when successful at Taunton and he followed up at Plumpton (3m1f; again good to soft); subsequent defeat, again under Murray Dodd, suggests others in here might be better treated.
runners.comment: Front-runner; hat-trick bid came up shy and others in here might be better treated

runners.name: Jukebox Fury
runners.spotlight: Last season he won 3m handicaps at Doncaster and Wetherby (both on good ground); his Ffos Las return was encouraging given the time he'd been absent and the soft conditions; in this scenario he's unexposed; considered.
runners.comment: Ffos Las return was encouraging given the time he'd been absent and the soft conditions

runners.name: Sleedagh
runners.spotlight: Last run too bad to be true but had been consistent in novice/maidens and the previous third at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) came in a strong race; the potential is there to be competitive off this mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: The potential is there to be competitive off this mark on handicap debut

runners.name: Gwennie May Star
runners.spotlight: Won races for Dan Skelton (2m1f/2m3f) and Olly Murphy (3m1f); now on a career-high mark, off which he was no match for the front two on stable debut at Bangor (2m7f).
runners.comment: On career-high mark, off which he was no match for the front two on stable debut at Bangor

runners.name: La Zoubida
runners.spotlight: Bumper and dual hurdles winner; penalties have been finding her out so handicaps now look a better option, but with her unraced beyond 2m5f there are some stamina doubts over this far.
runners.comment: Useful novice but with her unraced beyond 2m5f there are some stamina doubts over this far

runners.name: Whistle Stop Tour
runners.spotlight: Well beaten/pulled up over fences on his last two starts but has each-way claims on the pick of his form over hurdles this season and Gregor Walkingshaw gets a tune out of him.
runners.comment: Gregor Walkingshaw gets a tune out of him and returning to hurdles looks a good thing
runners.owner: Mrs Jo Tracey, K Alexander & Russell

runners.name: Wa Wa
runners.spotlight: Had lots of racing in Ireland before joining this yard last year; returned from a wind operation to win this 12 months ago off this very mark but he's an 11yo now and his last two runs, over fences and hurdles, weren't that encouraging.
runners.comment: Won this last year off the same mark but he ran no race last time at Warwick

runners.name: Go To War
runners.spotlight: After a brief stint over fences he got back on track in beating 14 rivals over 3m at Windsor (soft) under a positive ride; however, there was a further reminder of his inconsistency when he dropped away tamely at Haydock in February; what does heighten interest is a pretty good record when fresh.
runners.comment: Poor last time and inconsistent; his pretty good record when fresh heightens interest

runners.name: Highland Fashion
runners.spotlight: His first run at about 3m when winning at Newcastle (good to soft) and then ran second at Warwick; latest Musselburgh run wasn't too shoddy given he was held up off the pace before staying on and he reportedly bled that day; had a break since and wouldn't be fully exposed as a stayer.
runners.comment: Had a break since bleeding last time and he's still unexposed as a stayer

runners.name: Jupiter Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who was very well backed at Carlisle three weeks ago and ran out a ready winner (3m1f, good to soft); raised 6lb but appeals as a horse with more to offer.
runners.comment: Justified market support last time; raised 6lb but appeals as a horse with more to offer

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: He Knows Better
runners.spotlight: Went up 5lb for beating an exposed rival at Southwell (2m4f, soft; fine on good to soft) but he still ran well in a Class 3 at Newbury (2m6f) that didn't go entirely like clockwork; he could yet raise his bar higher; much respected.
runners.comment: Ran well to be third in a Class 3 at Newbury that didn't go entirely like clockwork
runners.owner: Mrs Isobel Webb & Mrs Julie Fowler

runners.name: Celestial Fashion
runners.spotlight: Shot up 16lb for her back-to-back wins (2m3f/2m5f) and, although a bad mistake was no help when last to finish at Carlisle, it could be that the handicapper has caught up with her.
runners.comment: Last to finish at Carlisle and it could be that the handicapper has caught up with her

runners.name: All Inn Hand
runners.spotlight: Won an uncompetitive novice hurdle (2m6f, good) last May and the following month made a successful chase debut at Hexham (3m, good to soft); a long absence is perhaps a greater concern than the 4lb rise.
runners.comment: Winning chase debut last June; long absence is perhaps a greater concern than the 4lb rise

runners.name: Solly Attwell
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins over similar trips to this last September and on good or yielding ground that he's not had in his last four races; however, current wellbeing is an issue and so too the blinkers in which he was tailed off last time when first fitted.
runners.comment: Out of form and much depends whether this return to better ground can inspire

runners.name: Dixie Mafia
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden over hurdles but ran respectably in his handicaps and this dual point winner could well do better over fences; has been off since January but one to consider, especially if at all strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Respectable runs in handicap hurdles; dual point winner who could do better over fences

races.race_name: Follow @racingtv On Instagram Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bold Light
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season; hasn't scaled the same heights this time round, although latest third of six at Newcastle was a bit more encouraging and he's well handicapped if staging a full-scale revival.
runners.comment: Not at best this term but not disgraced latest and well treated if staging revival

runners.name: Skyjack Hijack
runners.spotlight: On a roll in the second half of 2024, rattling off six straight wins (2m5f-3m1f), but pulled up on belated return at Kelso at the end of February; entitled to strip fitter but can still only watch.
runners.comment: Won six on the spin in 2024 but pulled up on belated reappearance in February

runners.name: The Dog Thief
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m maiden point at Alnwick in December and promising start over hurdles, chasing home a subsequent Grade 2 winner from the Willie Mullins yard at Carlisle before seeing off two next-time-out winners when landing a maiden at Market Rasen (2m4f, good to soft); a very interesting handicap newcomer.
runners.comment: Second to Grade 2 winner before seeing off next-time-out winners at Market R; promising

runners.name: Go Dante
runners.spotlight: Added to his excellent Sandown record when winning over 2m on soft in December; below par since, including a never-dangerous seventh when bidding for a third successive Imperial Cup back at Sandown, but this is the first time he's dropped to a Class 3 since winning at Cheltenham in December 2023; yard has won the last two runnings of this.
runners.comment: Below par since win in December but potential class act for yard with good record in this

runners.name: Serious Operator
runners.spotlight: 9yo who was tailed off at Kelso in February last time but that was the competitive Morebattle Hurdle over 2m; in good form over 2m4f prior to that, winning at Newcastle then second at Aintree (both good to soft); likely to be in the mix back up in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in Morebattle over 2m but in good form over 2m4f prior to that; should be in mix

runners.name: Jack Hyde
runners.spotlight: Didn't feature in the Imperial Cup (Go Dante two places ahead of him) but back on track with his sights lowered when shading a tight finish in 2m Ludlow handicap (good) 25 days ago, taking his hurdles record to 4-12; has won over this trip; should remain very competitive off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Fourth hurdle win when shading close finish at Ludlow latest; should go well again
runners.owner: Paddy Brennan Racing Syndicate Jack Hyde

runners.name: Nells Son
runners.spotlight: Better known as a chaser nowadays, but won twice over hurdles over four years ago; no real promise back hurdling six weeks ago but had wind surgery since and his stable is enjoying a cracking spring.
runners.comment: Below par back hurdling latest but had wind op since and stable enjoying a cracking spring

races.race_name: One Place. Every Race. Racing TV Novices' Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Mr Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Barely had to be nudged out to make a winning start to his handicap career at Exeter (2m2f, good) last Friday; the extra yardage here should help if anything and he'll take a bit of stopping under a 7lb penalty if showing up in a similar mood.
runners.comment: Easy win on Exeter handicap debut last Friday; will take a bit of stopping in  same mood

runners.name: King Kodiak
runners.spotlight: Improved third in 2m1f Carlisle novice (soft) in February; well treated on that form but hasn't managed to reproduce it in two subsequent handicaps, although he probably pressed for home too far out when third back at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) in refitted cheekpieces (retained) three weeks ago; remains one who could click from a lowly mark.
runners.comment: Well treated on novice third in March but been able to confirm it in two handicaps since

runners.name: Berkenshtaaap
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for a switch to handicap company when fourth of nine over 2m5f at Haydock (good to soft) seven weeks ago but it's still early days for a good stable with a very strong Perth record.
runners.comment: Only fair fourth on handicap debut but still early days for top yard which does well here

runners.name: Seeyainthesuninn
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler; has offered little in his three runs this term, weakening into a well-held seventh back from a break at Sedgefield 23 days ago.
runners.comment: Has offered little in three runs this term, including back from break this month

runners.name: Velvet Vulcan
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner who showed best hurdles form when going very close at Ayr (2m4f, soft) in January/February, latterly to The Coffey Boy; does need to shrug off two lesser runs since, though.
runners.comment: Two near misses at Ayr this year (to The Coffey Boy latterly) but below par last twice

runners.name: The Coffey Boy
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who made all and held on narrowly from Velvet Vulcan in conditional jockeys' race at Ayr (2m4f, soft) in February; not in the same form when fifth over 2m back at Ayr since but the return to 2m4f may see him in a better light.
runners.comment: Made all over 2m4f at Ayr; only fifth over 2m there since but may fare better back at 2m4f

runners.name: Walk On Son
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in maiden hurdles on his last two outings but showed a bit of promise in similar races prior to that and has had wind surgery ahead of this handicap debut; also refitted with the tongue-tie he wore in bumpers.
runners.comment: Disappointing last twice but undergone wind surgery ahead of handicap debut

runners.name: Valette
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three qualifying runs (trained in Ireland first two) but more realistic chance now handicapping for a yard which had a winner at Kelso on Monday.
runners.comment: Modest form in qualifying runs but more realistic chance now handicapping

runners.name: Zephlyn
runners.spotlight: Easily best effort in four maiden/novice hurdles last season when second at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft); did manage back-to-back wins over 2m on the Flat at Catterick last May but more recent AW Flat efforts have been no more than respectable.
runners.comment: Recent AW Flat efforts no more than respectable ahead of hurdle return

runners.name: Nae Bother At A'
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on 3m course handicap debut last June (final start for Gary Rutherford) and fared little better for new yard at Ayr (3m again) seven months later; hopes are pinned on a shorter trip helping after a further 101 days off.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 3m handicaps seven months apart; off for a further 101 days

races.race_name: One Place. Every Race. Racing TV Novices' Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Star Vantage
runners.spotlight: Only a respectable fourth of five at Sedgefield a fortnight ago, extending his losing run to 33; has a first-time visor added to his regular tongue-tie now.
runners.comment: Losing run at 33 and remains to be seen if new headgear combination helps to snap it

runners.name: Knockeranna Rose
runners.spotlight: Has reached the frame in two of his three handicaps at around 2m4f this spring and being eased another 2lb can only help.
runners.comment: Has reached frame in two of three handicaps and down another 2lb here

runners.name: Kap De Triomphe
runners.spotlight: Irish bumper winner who got off the mark over hurdles at the ninth attempt in a handicap at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) last month; not far off that form when fourth on his Warwick chase debut (2m4f, good) ten days later; enters calculations back hurdling.
runners.comment: Won hurdle last month and fair fourth in chase ten days later; enters calculations

runners.name: Humble Jumble
runners.spotlight: Won a Kelso bumper in November 2024 (2m, good to soft); fair third in a novice hurdle there (2m6f, good to soft) on second jumps start; hasn't reproduced it in two subsequent outings but may fare better now switching to a handicap.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; fair third on two of four hurdle starts; handicap debut

runners.name: Roi Du Roume
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in November; suffered heavy defeats in his first three hurdles but better signs after wind surgery when third of ten on handicap debut here (3m, good) on Wednesday, with the way he travelled suggesting this drop back in trip may help if anything.
runners.comment: Promise when third on handicap debut here (3m) on Wednesday, travelling well

runners.name: Gorvennec
runners.spotlight: Point winner; showed a bit in novice hurdles in November 2024 but tailed off in a novice hurdle and pulled up in a handicap chase in two outings this season over four months apart; fitted with first-time cheekpieces back hurdling; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Tailed off in hurdle and pulled up in chase this term; plenty to prove in new cheekpieces

runners.name: Dublinofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three qualifying runs over hurdles at up to 2m1f but did hint at promise on the first of two bumper starts and improvement wouldn't be a surprise now handicapping up in trip (bred for further).
runners.comment: Well held in three qualifying runs but potential improver now handicapping up in trip

runners.name: Poet's Gale
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a Galway bumper on debut; unconvincing hurdles form so far (including well held on completed start in handicaps) but Sean Bowen enlisted by an Irish yard no stranger to Perth success over the years, although not so much in recent times; betting interesting.
runners.comment: Unconvincing so far but Sean Bowen booked for Irish yard no stranger to Perth success

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kill Vanhowe
runners.spotlight: Did well in Irish points; runner-up in a 2m3f bumper at Cork on yielding ground but the winner was an 80-1 chance who hadn't shown much previously; this 5yo still rates a likely contender with improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Point winner; difficult to know what he achieved at Cork behind an 80-1 winner

runners.name: Ksar d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Bought for £85,000 after winning a 3m Irish point on yielding in October 2024 and that form has some substance; interesting to see what the market makes of him after such a long absence, especially with the yard doubly represented.
runners.comment: Bought for £85,000 after winning a 3m Irish point on yielding in October 2024

runners.name: Ar Ais Aris
runners.spotlight: Bought for £60,000 after finishing second on Irish point debut in November (yielding ground) and that form looks good with the third finishing fourth in the recent Grade 2 at Aintree; respected.
runners.comment: Bought for £60,000 after finishing second in a point and that form now reads well

runners.name: Rock On Ronnie
runners.spotlight: From off the pace he made a promising debut to be beaten 7.5l at Wetherby (soft) in December; second run has been a while coming but has to be of interest for last year's successful yard.
runners.comment: From off the pace he made a promising debut to be beaten 7.5l at Wetherby (soft) in Dec

runners.name: Fiskardo
runners.spotlight: Placed in two good-ground bumpers in 2024; easy to pick holes in the form of both races and perhaps the stable second string here; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Placed in good-ground bumpers in summer 2024 but not seen since; tongue-tie added now

runners.name: Effortless
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4.5l when third in a small-field Irish point in February (heavy); new yard not renowned for winning bumpers but this 4yo evidently has some ability.
runners.comment: Beaten 4.5l when third in a small-field Irish point in February (heavy)

runners.name: Hackley Bay
runners.spotlight: Unraced dam out of unraced half-sister to prolific French 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Master Flight; from a yard that's 0-34 in bumpers the last five seasons.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a yard that's 0-34 in bumpers the last five seasons

runners.name: Shane's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Jet Setting Johnny (2m4f chase) and Jet To Vegas (useful 2m/2m2f hurdle); Irish stable has bumper winners in Britain; market needs checking.
runners.comment: Irish stable has bumper winners in Britain; market needs checking

runners.name: Cockle Bay Wharf
runners.spotlight: 13,000euros foal; out of a bumper/2m7f hurdle winner; stable 1-26 in bumpers recent seasons.
runners.comment: Out of a bumper/2m7f hurdle winner; stable 1-26 in bumpers recent seasons

runners.name: Dolly Dior
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and never seriously counted on good ground at Sedgefield earlier this month; others preferred.
runners.comment: 40-1 and never seriously counted on good ground at Sedgefield earlier this month

runners.name: Betterthanadream
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Down Royal last month and at 50-1 not a lot was seemingly expected of her.
runners.comment: Tailed off at Down Royal last month and at 50-1 not a lot was seemingly expected of her

races.race_name: Grace And Dotty Handicap

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: Losing run stands at 17 but she has run some good races in defeat for Joey Ramsden and a strongly run race at this trip should see her to best effect; unraced on faster than good; each-way shout if the ground is no issue.
runners.comment: Strongly run 5f suits her best; went close on AW last month; each-way shout

runners.name: Masterclass
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run when the comfortable winner of a 5f handicap at Nottingham last May (5f, good to firm) off a 1lb higher mark; he was returning from a short break when fourth of 13 at Pontefract 17 days ago and that effort can be upgraded as he was wider than ideal as the field turned for home; each-way shout.
runners.comment: On handy mark and latest Pontefract fourth can be upgraded (wide on home turn); e-w shout

runners.name: Opal Storm
runners.spotlight: Produced late when winning a 5f handicap at Southwell three weeks ago; has the ability to defy a 3lb rise and she has run well over C&D in the past; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem and arrives on the back of an AW win; each-way claims once more

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins on turf last summer; on a competitive mark and she should come on for her recent return to action at Pontefract; seems to handle most ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: Should come on for her reappearance and on a fair mark on her 2025 best; respected

runners.name: Doralee
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f classified event at Redcar last summer; close third to Golden Prosperity at Catterick 16 days ago (5f, good), always prominent; needs a bit more if she's to come out on top in this competitive sprint.
runners.comment: Just one win from 17 starts but she arrives on the back of a good third at Catterick

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Tried over as far as 1m4f when trained by Martin Dunne; won a 7f event in Jersey last June; absent for 276 days prior to a close fourth at Wolverhampton last month (6f; first time in these retained accessories), an effort that can be marked up as she made a big move in the middle section of the race while racing on the outside of the field; dropping to 5f is new but a strongly run race at the trip could suit; suspicion a bigger effort is brewing.
runners.comment: Promising 4th on stable debut last month (6f, AW); a bigger effort could be brewing

runners.name: Trust Sergei
runners.spotlight: Progressive last spring, following a 5f AW win with a ready 6f win at this track (good to firm); mixed bag since then but his penultimate fourth at Wolverhampton was encouraging; failed to back it up ten days later but no doubt the ability is there and he's well handicapped now.
runners.comment: 6f win here last May off 2lb higher; promise two starts ago but couldn't back it up latest

runners.name: Thank The Lord
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two placed efforts on AW and he lines up here off a 6lb lower mark than when second at Newcastle in December; that is a result of four lesser efforts though and he now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two good runs but well short of that level so far in 2026; risky

runners.name: Beerwah
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 14th attempt when landing a 5f handicap at Beverley last September (soft) and ran well for fourth of 14 at Musselburgh one month later (good Topspeed figure); could progress further this year and he has shown promise on good to firm.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in good order; still feasibly weighted & he's one to consider back from a break

runners.name: Newyorkstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Veteran with a better record on AW than turf; 5f on fast ground no problem for him and he has run well after an absence in the past (although not last year); good run wouldn't be a surprise but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Conditions fine and on a dangerous mark so he can't be discounted despite his absence

runners.name: Ziva's Star
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and just one handicap run that makes her of interest, namely her close third at Ripon last July; absent for 139 days and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden with just one handicap run that makes her of interest; absent 139 days

runners.name: Lil Wade
runners.spotlight: Good runs have been few and far between since his Wolverhampton second in September 2024; easy enough to swerve.
runners.comment: Not much to shout about in the last 18 months and easy enough to swerve

runners.name: Tilsworth Ony Ta
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner but he has been short of his best on AW this year and the return to turf made no difference at Bath 19 days ago; not a solid option.
runners.comment: Five-time winner but out of sorts so far this year; not a solid option

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Ready win in a first-time visor at Newcastle last month (5f); perfectly good efforts in defeat twice since, coming from well back having blown the start at Newcastle on Monday (6f); more to prove on turf but this should be run to suit.
runners.comment: Three solid runs on AW since fitted with a visor; more to prove on turf but not ruled out

runners.name: Vault Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and well beaten at Newcastle when last seen in November; he was sent off favourite on that occasion and returns from a break off a 2lb lower mark; no doubt the ability is there to win races if the addition of a hood has a calming effect (often races too freely).
runners.comment: 12-race maiden but capable and he's tumbled down weights; dangerous if the new hood works

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly at Thirsk on Saturday (6f) but he had earlier won a 5f handicap at Catterick in determined fashion; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 5f Catterick win 16 days ago but ran poorly at Thirsk on Saturday (6f); others stronger

races.race_name: Summer Saturday Series Handicap

runners.name: Ey Up He's A Star
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (AW) in September and it was a close third at Thirsk (also 7f, good) on his return to turf a fortnight ago; drawn on the wing but can travel smoothly and should be in the thick of it off the same mark.
runners.comment: Close third (7f, good) on return to turf a fortnight ago; chance off same mark

runners.name: Wrydcroft
runners.spotlight: Won three of his last four, comprising two wins at Leicester (good to soft) and an AW success at Southwell (also 7f) on this month's comeback; big field no bother and he has to be of interest under a 3lb nudge.
runners.comment: Won three of his last four including AW return; has to be of interest under a 3lb nudge
runners.owner: G Libson And Mrs P Sly

runners.name: Reginald Charles
runners.spotlight: 0-16 on turf but he went mighty close at Redcar (good) in October; narrow winner at Southwell (also 7f, AW) on next start in December and it was a good effort when third to Wrydcroft over latter C&D last time; that did come after another two-month break but he's a player if backing it up 17 days on.
runners.comment: Running well with races spaced out; player if this doesn't come too soon

runners.name: Yehudi
runners.spotlight: 0-16 (includes turf) but has gone close a couple of times on AW; wore this headgear combination when fourth at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) under Hope Regan three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 0-16 (includes turf); had this headgear combination when fourth (AW) latest

runners.name: Onemorenomore
runners.spotlight: Good record here with three wins including a division of this (soft; acts on good/good to firm) in 2024; cashed in on reduced mark to open AW account over 7f in November and just 1lb higher on this return to turf after three months away; possibilities.
runners.comment: Won division of this in 2024; mark fine; possibilities

runners.name: Giant
runners.spotlight: Three wins (7f/1m, all AW) and it was a good effort when third in Southwell series final (1m, AW) 17 days ago; having a rare foray on grass but did finish second in good-ground Chester handicap (6f) in September; not dismissed from his lower turf mark.
runners.comment: Ran well latest (AW); rare foray on grass but not dismissed from his lower turf mark

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: In decent nick on AW for this yard since August, in the frame all seven starts and scoring at Newcastle (1m) in October; looked rusty on his turf reappearance a fortnight ago but he's a candidate to build on that; first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Rusty on turf return a fortnight ago; candidate to build on that; first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Ziggy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to handicaps when winning at Newcastle (7f) in September and bettered that form when third over 6f at Southwell (also AW) in November; had wind surgery before respectable comeback over latter C&D one month ago; unexposed on turf and worth a second look.
runners.comment: Respectable AW comeback last month; unexposed on turf and worth a second look

runners.name: Finn Ironside
runners.spotlight: Made virtually all in big-field York handicap (good to firm) in 2024; C&D (good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) and AW (also 7f) winner last summer; lesser efforts followed until a firm step back in the right direction last time and returns to turf from a handy mark; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner; step back in the right direction latest; considered

runners.name: Pearly Squirrel
runners.spotlight: C&D (good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) winner in July who also won 7f handicap (AW; no headgear) in March; missed the break (not for the first time) a fortnight ago but otherwise could have a say back on turf.
runners.comment: C&D winner who can miss the break; solid on AW of late; could have a say

runners.name: Sir David
runners.spotlight: Came good for Stella Barclay with last-gasp win at Carlisle (7f, good) in August but signed off for that yard with big soft-ground defeat the following month; no immediate encouragement on stable debut a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: No immediate encouragement on stable debut a fortnight ago

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: 1-2 (AW) as a juvenile and although winless last term her best efforts both came over C&D (good to firm); midfield finish (C&D, good to soft) after two-month break wasn't devoid of promise last time; interesting.
runners.comment: Likes it here and shaped like she can build on last month's C&D effort after a break

runners.name: Pianoforte
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win Bath novice (5f, good to firm) for Andrew Balding in June; 0-9 for this yard (majority 6f, turf/AW) and first-time cheekpieces (removed) didn't spark an improved effort last time; career-low mark and second go at this trip (first is easily excused) the straws to clutch at.
runners.comment: 0-9 for this yard; career-low mark and second go at this trip the straws to clutch at

runners.name: Born A Rebel
runners.spotlight: Can miss the break but scored twice last year, a 20-1 win at Newmarket (good to firm) in July supplemented by a Yarmouth (also 7f, good to soft) success in September; never dangerous on AW return in February but might do better this time.
runners.comment: Three turf wins; never dangerous on AW return in February but might do better this time

runners.name: Mecca's Symphony
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden whose reappearance third over C&D (good to firm) contained promise on this card last year; just one run subsequently and she's been off since wind surgery in June; unexposed and drops in grade for this comeback; needs considering, particularly if gaining market traction.
runners.comment: Promise on this card last year; unexposed and drops in grade for comeback; considered

runners.name: The New Bay Pearl
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Roger Varian but displayed clear ability on AW (tailed off on sole turf start) while also lacking consistency; sold for 5,000gns in December and wind surgery swiftly followed; market might help on this reappearance/stable debut.
runners.comment: Has ability but inconsistent; watch the market on reappearance/stable debut

races.race_name: Natural Innovations Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bunyola Bay
runners.spotlight: Finished third of eight, beaten 3l, when the well-backed favourite for a 6.5f maiden at Newbury last October (soft; winner has since finished fourth in the Greenham); could see his race out more strongly on the sounder surface; big player.
runners.comment: Well backed when third in a Newbury maiden (6.5f, soft) last October; open to improvement

runners.name: Moreedd
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree, closely related to three winners, notably Group 1 winner Benbatl (RPR 124) and a half-brother to three other winners including 1000 Guineas heroine Elmalka (113); dam a Grade/Group 1 winner over 1m2f (118); five of those six winning siblings were successful first time out; of obvious interest.
runners.comment: Top-class pedigree; represents a powerful stable and is of obvious interest on paper

runners.name: Celtic Chariot
runners.spotlight: Fetched 55,000gns as a foal and 45,000gns as a yearling but went through the ring for just 1,000gns last October; second foal; half-brother to Canadian 7f AW winner Vrana; dam French Listed-placed 6.5f 2yo winner (RPR 90); likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 45,000gns yearling; sold unraced for 1,000gns last October; likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Glen Nevis
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns yearling; second foal of an 8.6f/1m3f winner (including US; RPR 98), herself a half-sister to winners Gifts Of Gold (smart 1m1f-1m7f) and Dusky Queen (7f Listed); appealing pedigree and no surprise to see him play a part on debut.
runners.comment: 240,000gns yearling; dam a useful and well-related winner; can play a part on debut
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Balmoral Boy
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two runs at Newcastle this spring (1m/7f); likely to find life easier once handicapping.
runners.comment: Minor promise in two AW runs (1m/7f) this spring; handicaps will provide better openings

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Silver State
runners.spotlight: He made an instant impact once handicapping in a visor, winning over 8.6f at Wolverhampton in February and following up over 1m at Newcastle 11 days later; back on the up with an emphatic front-running success at Bath last week (1m, good to soft) and he is 2lb well in under a 6lb penalty; faster ground to contend with today but this will be his last chance in a Class 5 for a while and he has to be high on the list.
runners.comment: 3-5 since handicapping and he bolted up at Bath last week; faster ground today; contender

runners.name: Toastmaster
runners.spotlight: Seeking his first win but he has run well in defeat in all three handicap starts (9.4f-1m3f); drops back to 1m today and he could still have a bit more to offer.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden but he's run well in all three handicaps and the drop to 1m may help

runners.name: Booziebrunch
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his 2yo efforts when finishing second to a handicap blot at Southwell three weeks ago (1m); should give his running but perhaps vulnerable to less-exposed sorts.
runners.comment: Good second on his seasonal return this month; perhaps vulnerable to less-exposed sorts

runners.name: Welbury
runners.spotlight: Well on top at the finish of a 1m novice at Southwell on his seasonal return five weeks ago; that was his first attempt at the trip and he moves into handicaps with potential and his stable going well.
runners.comment: Strong at the finish in a 1m AW novice last month; more to come in handicaps; yard in form

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: Free-going sort; not matched 2yo form (for Harry Eustace) in three runs for new connections and he looked a hard ride at Wolverhampton last month, hanging badly right throughout; risky on the back of that.
runners.comment: Yet to fire for this yard and he looked a hard ride last time; risky on the back of that

runners.name: Tarmonbarry Kid
runners.spotlight: Promising debut at Chester last July (7f, good; form worked out); no progress in two 7f runs at Newcastle afterwards but he has been gelded prior to his handicap/seasonal debut and stepping up to 1m should suit; one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Of interest on his turf debut last summer; 1m should suit now handicapping; shortlisted

runners.name: Saxophonist
runners.spotlight: Two third-place finishes last season, both over 7f on AW; bred to be useful and market support would look significant on this seasonal and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise twice on AW last year; bred to be useful; check betting on handicap/seasonal debut

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Saliko
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Nottingham last July (1m2f, good and good to soft); just 1lb higher than for the second of those victories but she ended 2025 with a poor run and may not want the ground too lively.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f Nottingham wins last July; fair mark if ready to roll after six months off

runners.name: Alkumatic Jo Jo
runners.spotlight: In the frame in her four 2yo runs (7f-1m); only midfield on her seasonal debut 12 days ago but stepping up in trip could spark something extraa.
runners.comment: Promising at up to 1m as a 2yo; midfield on return 12 days ago; now steps up in trip

runners.name: Little Miss India
runners.spotlight: Often slowly away but it hasn't prevented her from winning three times on the AW since October (9.4f-1m4f); this trip more suitable than the 1m she tackled at Bath 19 days ago; untried on faster than good.
runners.comment: Three AW wins since October (9.4f-1m4f); untried on faster than good; often slowly away

runners.name: Percy's Daydream
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a 3yo, winning at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) and Leicester (1m4f, good); below par on her final start in 2025 but she reportedly boiled over in the preliminaries; still has low mileage; market useful with the stable also running Myrrh.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025 until a low-key run in September; trainer runs two here; market useful

runners.name: Finally Escaped
runners.spotlight: Fitted with headgear for the first time at Thirsk two weeks ago (1m, good; turf debut) and ran her best race so far, keeping on for a never-dangerous sixth of 13 (beaten 3.75l); should stay this trip and she is open to further progress.
runners.comment: Plugged on for sixth in a 1m handicap at Thirsk two weeks ago; unexposed; 1m2f should suit

runners.name: Oh Yes You Do
runners.spotlight: Had subsequent winners back in second and third when scoring at Leicester in October (1m, good to soft), her second start; still looked to be learning her job there and she is open to further progress this year; 1m2f seems likely to suit her well; strong contender.
runners.comment: Still looked green when winning a 1m maiden in October; more to come in handicaps in 2026

runners.name: Myrrh
runners.spotlight: Improved her RPR with each of her three 2yo runs, winning at Hamilton in September (8.3f, soft; second past the post) and running well for third at Kempton one month later (1m; penalised); some encouragement for 1m2f on pedigree; stable also run Percy's Daydream.
runners.comment: Improved with each run as a 2yo; steps up in trip for handicap debut; stable run two here

runners.name: Nanny Park
runners.spotlight: 1m AW win as a 2yo; consistent last year without looking ahead of her mark; sold out of James Fanshawe's stable for 18,000gns in October; she will need to be at the top of her game to make a winning stable debut after 193 days off.
runners.comment: Consistent rather than progressive for J Fanshawe; sold 18,000gns since latest; vulnerable

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Jackal
runners.spotlight: He made a pleasing debut, as predicted by the betting, when second of nine at Wolverhampton last month (6f); couldn't cope with the easy winner on that occasion but that rival has since gone on to win a Newmarket handicap off a BHA mark of 85; open to improvement; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Pleasing 2nd to rival who has boosted the form since in 6f AW maiden last month; e-w shout

runners.name: Bucklow Hill
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a Group 3 winner; justified market support when overcoming inexperience to win a 6f maiden at Lingfield (AW) in January; open to improvement and he can make a bold bid despite his 7lb penalty; withdrawn after playing up in the preliminaries at Newcastle last month.
runners.comment: Looked a useful prospect with win in a 6f AW maiden in January; key player despite penalty

runners.name: Wintercast
runners.spotlight: 700,000gns yearling; first foal of a 7f 2yo/1m4f winner (including Listed; RPR 107), herself a sister to 1m4f Listed winner Rainbow Sky; there is also some speed in the bottom line of his pedigree but, even so, longer trips should suit better in time; still warrants a close market check on debut.
runners.comment: 700,000gns yearling; first foal of a Listed winner from a good family; market to guide

runners.name: Azure Point
runners.spotlight: 44,000euros foal, 45,000gns yearling; sixth foal; closely related to 6f winner Pure Artistry (RPR 62), half-sister to winners Epic Pass (7.5f-1m2f including AW/Germany; 81) and Upepo (1m4f/1m5f AW; 77); dam 5f-1m winner (including Listed; 105); enough paper appeal to mean he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: 45,000gns yearling with three winning siblings; dam a Listed winner; market check advised

runners.name: Wayward Queen
runners.spotlight: Second foal of a 5f 2yo winner (including Group 3; RPR 100); starts out in a winnable race and she needs a look in the market.
runners.comment: Dam a Group 3 winner as a 2yo; starts out at a realistic level; betting to guide

runners.name: King's Road Dandy
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing third of eight in a Pontefract maiden (6f; good to firm) 261 days ago; open to improvement but it will be required.
runners.comment: Third of eight in a 6f maiden at Pontefract last August; can do better but he needs to

runners.name: Flight Control
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling; sold unraced for 8,000gns last October; first foal of a modest maiden from the family of Rock Of Gibraltar; likely best watched.
runners.comment: 200,000euros yearling but sold unraced for 8,000gns last October; best watched today

runners.name: Space Party
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros foal, £55,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to 5f winner Ninety Nine (RPR 70); dam a Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (96), out of 5f/6f winning half-sister to Group 3 winners Silca's Gift (7f) and Violette (6f); two of the trainer's four runners this year (all in the last eight days) have been successful; worth a market check.
runners.comment: £55,000 breeze-up 2yo; good sprinting pedigree; worth a market check

runners.name: Masaban
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two runs for Dermot Weld last summer (7f, yielding/1m, AW); gelded and sold for 20,000euros in November; bred to do much better and he makes his return with his new stable going well; he's an interesting 3yo but the drop to 6f isn't sure to suit on paper; betting should guide.
runners.comment: Can leave 2yo form behind this year but 6f a query ahead of this stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Dontwaryboutathing
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two 6f runs 323 days apart; minor handicaps beckon.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 6f runs 323 days apart; minor handicaps beckon

runners.name: Rousham
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard, despite wearing a hood, and was always in rear in Newcastle maiden (7f, AW; 125-1) last month; withdrawn after playing up in the stalls at Pontefract this month; can't recommend.
runners.comment: Pulled hard and finished out the back in a 7f AW maiden last month; no appeal

runners.name: Kodi K
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two maidens (6.5f and 6f) on slow ground last autumn; today's faster ground needs to spark a transformation.
runners.comment: Low-level form in two slow-ground maidens last autumn; faster ground not enough to tempt

runners.name: Roy Lane
runners.spotlight: Couldn't land a blow in a 5f novice (good to firm; weak 15-2 chance) at last season's Dante meeting; absent for 345 days, during which time he has been gelded; has a fine sprinting pedigree so a big step forward would come as no great surprise.
runners.comment: Last of six on York debut last May; gelded since; bred to be smart and may do much better
runners.owner: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: 33-1, green and always in rear on his Redcar debut last June (6f, good to firm); gelded since; likely one for the longer term.
runners.comment: 33-1, green and well beaten in a 6f novice last June; been gelded; longer-term prospect

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Berkshire Schmokin
runners.spotlight: Two turf starts last year; made the breakthrough in his second handicap when scoring at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in February; yet to build on that, however, and others arguably have more scope for improvement on this switch back to grass.
runners.comment: Won 9.5f AW h'cap in February; others arguably have more scope for improvement
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Tommo's Ginjaninja
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when second at Southwell (1m, AW) in March; interesting to see if he can build on that up in trip for this turf and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Improved 2nd on AW last time; may build on that up in trip for turf and handicap debut

runners.name: Risk Averse Rebel
runners.spotlight: Fairly solid efforts in three AW handicaps last month but others have more scope for improvement on this switch back to turf.
runners.comment: Fairly solid efforts in three AW handicaps last month; others more likely to improve

runners.name: Kiss For An Angel
runners.spotlight: Tapped back into his initial promise when close second of five in Redcar nursery (1m, good) in November; gelded ahead of this comeback and he's another whose pedigree suggests this trip can unlock more.
runners.comment: Close second in 1m nursery (good) in November; pedigree says this trip is the go; gelded

runners.name: Frustration
runners.spotlight: 80-1 for her handicap debut and was beaten 18l at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) last month; others have more compelling cases.
runners.comment: 80-1 for her handicap debut and beaten 18l at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) last month

runners.name: Rumbustious
runners.spotlight: Narrowly recorded his best effort when staying on for third on return at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last month; brings potential to this handicap debut with turf no problem on pedigree.
runners.comment: Best effort when 3rd (1m2f, AW) last time; turf no problem on pedigree; h'cap debut

runners.name: Brave Leader
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement in three qualifying runs around 1m (good/AW) last year; pedigree a pointer to him doing better over this longer trip on handicap debut/reappearance; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Steady improver last term; pedigree says this trip helps on h'cap debut/return; gelded

runners.name: Cotton Bud
runners.spotlight: Steady progression in four starts for Kevin Philippart De Foy and left that yard for £26,000gns after finishing second in Nottingham nursery (about 1m, good) in October; new trip a positive on pedigree and market should help on this reappearance and stable debut.
runners.comment: Nursery second when last seen in October; new trip a positive on pedigree; stable debut

runners.name: Madjid
runners.spotlight: Big chunk of improvement when fourth at Southwell (1m, AW) in January; switches back to turf (sole run easily forgiven) and he's interesting up in trip on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Big chunk of improvement latest; one of many who's interesting up in trip on h'cap debut
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

races.race_name: Welcome To Cork Racecourse Mallow Maiden

runners.name: Suspicious Mindz
runners.spotlight: Best effort a close third in Listed heat at Tipperary (7.5f, good; dual Group 1 runner-up Brussels second); poor effort next time on soft at Roscommon; gelded ahead of 7f Dundalk maiden third in October; big player on return with drying ground in his favour.
runners.comment: Close third in Listed at Tipperary last year (Brussels second) is solid form; enjoys good

runners.name: Nil Bua Gan Dua
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; promise when fourth on debut at the Curragh; not disgraced in 1m Leopardstown Group 2 on second start; ran to similar level when beaten 6l behind smart Ballydoyle colt in 1m1f Leopardstown Group 3; fair chance with sights lowered on return; holds Irish Derby entry.
runners.comment: Posted good efforts in Group company last year; sets the standard dropping down in class
runners.owner: A K Whelan & Reeves Thoroughbred Racing

runners.name: Tashakour
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging Curragh debut last July, staying on late over 7f; failed to improve over same C&D following month; best effort yet when runner-up to Ballydoyle colt at Navan (1m) on return last month but that was on heavy; has race-fitness on his side.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts at the Curragh; best run at Navan on latest but was on heavy; race-fit
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Chamonix
runners.spotlight: Son of Wootton Bassett cost 100,000euros as a yearling; weak in the betting on debut (20-1) when fifth of eight at the Curragh (1m1f, soft to heavy) three weeks ago; beaten only 3.75l and improvement can be expected.
runners.comment: 100,000euros yearling was fifth of eight at the Curragh on debut but not beaten far

runners.name: Coasting
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath gelding cost 20,000euros; went modest fourth behind stablemate and favourite Allsortz over 6f on debut at Dundalk a fortnight ago; bred to appreciate the step up in trip on turf debut; in the mix.
runners.comment: Modest fourth behind stablemate/favourite Allsortz over 6f at Dundalk; bred to stay trip

runners.name: Oust
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier colt cost 40,000euros; backed in from big prices (sent off 10-1) when 8l fourth of seven on 7f Dundalk debut in December; will need to improve significantly to take this.
runners.comment: 8l fourth of seven on 7f Dundalk debut in December; will need to improve significantly

runners.name: Fallacy
runners.spotlight: Bred for the AW but didn't show a whole pile on 1m Dundalk debut in October although bit better over same C&D when sixth of 14 in December; gelded; turf debut; stable in form.
runners.comment: Bred for the AW but didn't show a whole pile at Dundalk last year; turf debut; gelded

runners.name: Roaming Coyote
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds and never a factor on 1m Curragh debut in October; safe to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds and never a factor on 1m Curragh debut in October; look elsewhere

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Race

runners.name: The Publican's Son
runners.spotlight: Belied 40-1 odds with strong-finishing second in Curragh Group 3 (6f, good to yielding) where he was the sole newcomer in the field; inexperience costly when in rear in the Middle Park; slowly away when respectable fifth in Leopardstown Group 3 on return 12 days ago; bred to get the mile and could be a class apart.
runners.comment: Clearly highly thought of as yet to run outside of Group company; bred to get a mile

runners.name: Brownstown
runners.spotlight: Won Naas barrier trial last May; successful track debut at Leopardstown (7f, soft) in June; failed to make a major impact in good-class contests thereafter, on latest when midfield in the Goffs Million; entitled to go well at this level and fresh may be the time to catch her.
runners.comment: Won barrier trial and debut; entitled to go well and fresh may be the time to catch her

runners.name: Why Wyoming
runners.spotlight: Intello filly; 17,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners Dance Of Life (French 10.5f; RPR 89) and Agrimonia (Swiss 1m1f/1m2f); dam 5f 2yo winner (99); tough task on debut.
runners.comment: Intello filly; 17,000euros yearling; half-sister to two winners; tough task on debut

runners.name: Star Strewn
runners.spotlight: Promise in two Dundalk runs for Cormac Farrell; impressive winning debut for this yard at Dundalk (7f) in February; finished last of eight when pitched into Listed company at Lingfield on latest; turf debut.
runners.comment: Won 7f Dundalk maiden in style but finished last in Listed at Lingfield; turf debut

runners.name: Loch Gamhna
runners.spotlight: Nondescript run at Naas on debut last September; promising third at the Curragh following month (soft to heavy; form worked out well); perhaps needed the run when below-par at the Curragh last month.
runners.comment: Each-way player judged on Curragh third last year and possibly needed the run last month

runners.name: Echo Of Faith
