runners.spotlight: Left previous form behind when winning Roscommon maiden in August (7.5f, good), value for more as had to wait for a gap; weakened badly on AW debut at Dundalk in February; likely to come up short in this company; blinkers.
runners.comment: Roscommon maiden winner on good; ran no race at Dundalk on return; this is tough

runners.name: Dial Me In
runners.spotlight: Fourth in Naas barrier trial before successful track debut in 7f Leopardstown auction event (good) last summer, overcoming traffic problems to get up on post; ran below that level when beaten odds-on favourite at Killarney (1m, soft) in July; 282 days off; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Won on track debut at Leopardstown on good; well below that on soft at Killarney; absence

runners.name: Zia Zabel
runners.spotlight: Made 200,000euros as a foal but only 100,000gns as a yearling; promising Curragh debut second but failed to build on that when favourite at Leopardstown; off the track 188 days.
runners.comment: Promising Curragh debut second but failed to build on that when favourite at Leopardstown

runners.name: Sursum Corda
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced despite finishing last of six at Leopardstown (sent off 150-1), beaten 7l in a good contest, but can only be watched; 343 days off.
runners.comment: 150-1 for debut and finished last, although beaten only 7l; can only be watched here

races.race_name: Follow Cork Racecourse On Social Media Handicap

runners.name: Notforalongtime
runners.spotlight: Recorded his ninth AW win over 1m in November but held of late on same surface; runner-up in this race last year off 7lb higher so demands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Prolific AW winner runner-up in this race last year off 7lb higher so demands respect

runners.name: Magna Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: First reserve; AW winner in Britain (9.5f); okay form for current yard on AW at end of last year and no disgrace in recent turf return; drop in trip here may not be in his favour though.
runners.comment: No disgrace in recent turf return; drop in trip may not be in his favour though; reserve

runners.name: Nibras Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four career wins include at Dundalk last November for Gavin Cromwell; joined new yard since last run in January and may just need this.
runners.comment: Joined new yard since last run in January and may just need this

runners.name: Brosna Green
runners.spotlight: No success in 17 starts, most at Dundalk; first turf run since last August and of limited appeal.
runners.comment: No success in 17 starts, most at Dundalk; first turf run since August so of limited appeal

runners.name: Jasmine Affanalis
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a positive note when winning at Tipperary (1m1f, yielding) off 57; two just okay AW runs on last month's return so much more needed on turf return.
runners.comment: Needs to improve on both recent AW runs

runners.name: Shahada Ace
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a 7.5f Tipperary handicap last August but wasn't as good raised 13lb at Bellewstown next time; soft ground wasn't ideal on recent Gowran return so more expected back on better ground here.
runners.comment: Soft ground not ideal on recent Gowran return so more expected back on better ground

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins came at this trip on a sound surface; on a long losing run (over 9l off Launch Time at Leopardstown on latest) and down to basement mark for the first time.
runners.comment: Down to basement mark for the first time in his career so worth a look

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; narrow winner of a 6f Dundalk maiden in February but disqualified due to a technicality; okay run on latest of two handicap runs since over same C&D; cheekpieces added for turf debut and interesting if getting a run.
runners.comment: Potential improver on turf debut with cheekpieces added; reserve

runners.name: Launch Time
runners.spotlight: Reappeared a fortnight ago to get off the mark over this trip at Leopardstown in first-time cheekpieces, defying a wide draw to win a shade snugly; 7lb rise a bit harsh but no surprise if he handled it.
runners.comment: Leopardstown winner up 7lb but could well handle it

runners.name: Peig Sayers
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo absent since always trailing last of 22 on handicap debut at the Curragh in October; better ground may suit but very hard to be confident in.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo very hard to be confident in on seasonal return

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has done most of his racing at Dundalk; recent turf return (sixth to Launch Time at Leopardstown, beaten 3.25l) was a solid run but more required.
runners.comment: Recent turf return was a solid run but more required
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: River Derwent
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner and last of two turf wins came in Roscommon handicap nearly two years ago (off 61); AW form over the winter wasn't bad so can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: AW form over the winter wasn't bad so can't be ruled out off a decent mark

runners.name: Johnny's Oasis
runners.spotlight: Dual Dundalk AW winner is 0-8 on turf and recent AW form none too encouraging for this turf return; wide draw a negative.
runners.comment: Recent AW form none too encouraging for this turf return; wide draw a negative

runners.name: Reponse Finale
runners.spotlight: One of her better efforts came here last September when third over 6f; subsequent AW form not at same level; absent since September and may need this.
runners.comment: Decent run here last year at 6f; lacks a recent run so may need this

runners.name: He's Holding Court
runners.spotlight: Three Dundalk runs over the winter before handicap/turf debut back in trip at Naas last month, showing up for long way before dropping out; up in trip here and 2lb out of handicap.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo up in trip here and 2lb out of handicap

runners.name: She's On The Ball
runners.spotlight: No impact in a fillies' maiden for Clive Cox last year and hasn't shown much in two runs for current yard this year; handicap debutante is 4lb wrong and yard have a more likely contender in Notforalongtime.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap debutante and yard have a more likely contender in Notforalongtime

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe claimer last summer but underwhelming subsequently in handicaps for this yard; starts off this season off 14lb lower mark and able to drop in grade so not to be ruled out.
runners.comment: Regressive last season but down 14lb and able to drop in grade so don't rule out

runners.name: Swiss Army Officer
runners.spotlight: Three career wins include over C&D way back; hasn't won in nearly three years but good second on final start last year at Killarney in October when 8lb out of the weights; hard to assess but on a nice mark and good draw.
runners.comment: Last seen when second at Killarney; nice mark and good draw so respect

races.race_name: Social Packs From Just 30 Euros Maiden

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ulysses colt cost 67,000gns as a yearling; Irish Derby entry; weak in the betting late on when respectable fourth of 13 at Navan (1m2f, heavy) on debut this month; yard 33-94 at the track; obvious player in new headgear.
runners.comment: Good Navan fourth on debut when weak in betting; yard 33-94 at the track; obvious player

runners.name: Enceladus
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of promise when third on his debut at Punchestown (1m1f, yielding to soft) before finishing third at Naas (1m, yielding) in rematch with Cape Cod who won Leopardstown handicap on return off 92 this month; holds Irish Derby entry and entitled to improve up in trip this season.
runners.comment: Two solid runs last year and looks strong form on a line through Cape Cod; every chance

runners.name: Eternal Echo
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and third at Clonmel (9.5f) on sole Flat start for Gordon Elliott but has struggled in three starts for this yard, including on Flat 15 days ago at Gowran.
runners.comment: Third at Clonmel (9.5f) on sole Flat start for Elliott but has struggled for this yard

runners.name: Theorist
runners.spotlight: Left the impression he would be suited by a step up in trip when not beaten far twice over 1m at Dundalk last year; one to consider on his return in first-time tongue-tie; definite candidate on turf debut.
runners.comment: Left the impression he would be suited by this trip when not beaten far over 1m at Dundalk

runners.name: Reynir
runners.spotlight: Siyouni colt is half-brother to the useful Reyenzi; sent off 100-30 for 9.5f debut at Gowran 15 days ago but all chance lost at the start with a slow break; yard won two of last five runnings of this race, including last year; has to be watched for market support.
runners.comment: 100-30 for debut at Gowran but slow break; yard won 2 of last 5 runnings of this race
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Almazann
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to 1m2f winner Alazeyna (RPR 93); dam 1m4f winner (inc Listed; 110), half-sister to 1m6f/2m4f Group 1 winner Alandi and 1m4f Listed winner Aliyfa, out of 1m4f Listed winning sister to disqualified Oaks winner Aliysa; Irish Derby entry; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m2f winner Alazeyna (RPR 93); dam 1m4f winner (inc Listed); shortlisted
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Confused
runners.spotlight: Calyx gelding; 52,000gns yearling; half-brother to Italian 8.7f 2yo winner Ancient Story; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 75), sister to 1m4f/13.5f winner Moon Daisy, half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Mountain Bear; market the best guide.
runners.comment: Calyx gelding; 52,000gns yearling; dam 1m2f winner; market the best guide

runners.name: Strike De Vega
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; 44,000euros yearling; closely related to My Kurkum (Group-placed 1m AW 2yo/1m1f turf; RPR 93), half-brother to 6f AW 2yo winner; dam 9.5f/1m2f winner, half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Shaman; watch.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; 44,000euros yearling; closely related to My Kurkum (Group-placed); watch

runners.name: Cut The Rope
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; 0-12 over hurdles and rated 112; solid run on the Flat when sixth of 23 at the Curragh in November (1m4f, heavy); this trip may be on the sharp side but each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose; solid run on the Flat when sixth of 23 at the Curragh in November (heavy)
runners.owner: Brian P Whelan & Thomas J Quinn

runners.name: Big Dispute
runners.spotlight: Rated 104 over hurdles; debut Killarney bumper winner in 2023; hit the frame last three maiden hurdles in 2025; Flat debut for Kevin Coleman; market will guide.
runners.comment: Hit the frame last 3 maiden hurdles in 2025; Flat debut for Coleman; market will guide

runners.name: Leeward
runners.spotlight: Dwelt coming out of the stalls when tailed off at the Curragh (1m2f) on debut last month; stable second string on riding arrangements.
runners.comment: Tailed off at the Curragh on debut; stable second string on riding arrangements

runners.name: Musical Master
runners.spotlight: Sent off at odds of 50-1 for 9.5f Gowran debut this month and was never involved; will need more time.
runners.comment: Sent off at odds of 50-1 for 9.5f Gowran debut and was never involved; will need more time

runners.name: The Rock Factor
runners.spotlight: Change of emphasis after Naas bumper run in January; solid Flat pedigree but only modest late headway when 18l behind the winner at Navan (1m) last month; this trip should at least help.
runners.comment: 18l behind on Flat debut last month; this trip should at least help; watch betting

runners.name: Silver Cuff
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a single rival home on his 1m2f Leopardstown debut nine days ago; leave out of calculations.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a single rival home on his debut nine days ago; leave out of calculations

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; soundly beaten both runs over hurdles and similar story in two tries on Flat; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten both runs over hurdles and similar story in 2 tries on Flat; hard to fancy

runners.name: A Man's Horse
runners.spotlight: Late starter made 25,000euros as a foal; large odds and made no impression twice at the Curragh last year; watch.
runners.comment: Late starter made 25,000euros; large odds and made no impression twice at the Curragh

races.race_name: Dine In Our Panoramic Restaurant Race

runners.name: Limestone
runners.spotlight: Progressed nicely in each of three juvenile runs, bumping into smart Ballydoyle colts on first two before landing Listowel rated race in September, making all on heavy ground (1m); should relish middle-distances this year (Derby entered) and fitted with a tongue-tie for seasonal return.
runners.comment: Progressive staying 2yo last year; should relish middle-distances and big shout here

runners.name: Summer Is Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Both wins last year came at 7f, in Down Royal maiden (good) and needed every yard when getting up late in Roscommon winners contest on soft, having previously finished third in very valuable Naas auction contest over inadequate 6f; didn't land a blow in Goffs Million (Curragh) final start and takes a step up in trip here on seasonal return from a yard in hot form; rider's claim negates his double penalty.
runners.comment: Upped in trip on seasonal return; rider's claim negates his double penalty

runners.name: Mr Vettori
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega colt improved nicely in three runs at two, notably chasing home the high-class Hawk Mountain in Curragh maiden and final start outstayed long-time leader at Gowran in testing conditions, tending to hang right both occasions; Irish Derby entry looks the type to keep improving and considered.
runners.comment: Gowran winner an Irish Derby entry who looks the type to keep improving and considered

runners.name: Port Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt is out of Group 1-winning miler Amazing Maria; successful on only 2yo start, hampered and hanging badly but doing well under a considerate ride in getting up late in 7f Naas maiden on softish ground; dual Derby entry should appreciate this step up in trip and could be anything.
runners.comment: Naas winner on only 2yo start; bred to relish this trip and could be anything
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

races.race_name: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Handicap

runners.name: Ben Lawers
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 1m AW maiden at Dundalk in February and beaten under 2l in valuable Lingfield AW handicap early this month over same trip; barely gets 1m2f so potentially vulnerable back on the turf.
runners.comment: Best recent AW form at 1m; barely gets 1m2f so potentially vulnerable back on the turf

runners.name: Akecheta
runners.spotlight: Gained second heavy ground win in conditions race at Gowran (7f) last September; yet to win a handicap, well held both starts this term at the Curragh (well-backed favourite for the Irish Lincoln on return), never counting over 1m2f latest; bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Two disappointing Curragh runs of late; bit to prove, especially at this trip

runners.name: Iron Fist
runners.spotlight: Two soft ground wins last term included 9.5f Clonmel handicap off 84; didn't count back at 1m on recent seasonal return at the Curragh but entitled to come on for that so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent comeback run and trip to suit

runners.name: Final Voyage
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, last win at Dundalk in February 2024 off 3lb higher; some good efforts last year, including over this trip on the AW, and creditable run in the circumstances (slowly away) at the Curragh on latest under today's rider; one to consider.
runners.comment: Did well considering slow start on latest; one to consider

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner (twice at 1m2f); out of form on turf last season but some good runs this term, good second over inadequate 7f at Naas (soft) and nine days ago at Leopardstown when coming late to dead-heat for fourth with Amplitude over this trip, having not had the clearest of runs; better ground here to suit and has to be on the shortlist with rider now claiming 7lb.
runners.comment: Dead-heated for fourth with Amplitude at Leopardstown; better ground to suit

runners.name: Bay Colony
runners.spotlight: Highly tried on occasions, sole win coming in 1m4f Limerick fillies' maiden a year ago; comeback run early this month was full of promise, staying on fourth (fifth won a Group 3 next time) of 18 behind a well handicapped winner over this trip at the Curragh; ideally wants 1m4f but on a nice mark and could well play a major role.
runners.comment: Curragh return full of promise; on a nice mark and could well play a major role

runners.name: Mano Chicago
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden shaped well initially in 2yo maidens; missed his entire 3yo campaign and reappeared to chase home a clear winner in 9.5f Gowran maiden; entitled to come on for that although needs to in order to justify this rating on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Long-absent before Gowran maiden second; remains potential improver on handicap debut
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Calm The Jets
runners.spotlight: Landed a 1m maiden at Dundalk on debut impressively in January 2024; off the track (gelded) until never landing a blow in midfield in quite valuable Curragh handicap over same trip early this month (2l off Final Voyage, ahead of Iron Fist); entitled to come on for that and remains unexposed on only third career start and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent run and remains unexposed on only third career start

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Rounded off last term in with emphatic win in 9.5f Gowran maiden (heavy) and similar level of form on second start back when dead-heating for fourth with Quatre Bras in Leopardstown handicap over this trip nine days ago; respected.
runners.comment: Dead-heated for fourth with Quatre Bras at Leopardstown; contender

runners.name: Eben Zaabeel
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden in Britain for Charlie Hills, runner-up in AW handicaps final two starts; never sighted on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse early this month, probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: AW runner-up in Britain; no show on recent hurdles debut, likely best watched for now

runners.name: Genealogy
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m for Aidan O'Brien at two; easily pick of three hurdle runs for this yard when second in Ascot handicap (soft), most recently never counted on Flat return in 1m6f Curragh conditions event early this month; cheekpieces now tried and back in trip on return to handicap company.
runners.comment: 1m6f too far on latest; cheekpieces tried and back in trip on return to handicap company

runners.name: Defiantly
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo didn't debut until June last year, winning Killarney auction maiden on second start; contested 1m6f Listed races both subsequent starts, staying on fourth at Leopardstown and never landed a blow when beaten 5.5l at Ascot in October; drops to handicap company for seasonal return and has to be respected.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo drops to handicap company for seasonal return and has to be respected

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: Decent performer in France, eight wins include in Listed company; never sighted on yard debut over an inadequate 1m at the Curragh early this month, more expected here at his optimum trip.
runners.comment: French Listed winner; inadequate trip on yard debut, more expected now at best trip

runners.name: Coeur d'Or
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner but not since Irish Cambridgeshire win of 2024; definite encouragement on second start back off reduced mark without quite staying this trip at Leopardstown; blinkers now replace visor and now 11lb below career-high mark but stamina remains a concern at this trip.
runners.comment: Down to a nice mark but remains vulnerable at this trip
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: Westminster Moon
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner in Poland at up to 1m4f and placed in German Group 1 and Italian Group 2 in 2024; not fired since joining current yard in seven starts, most recently quite valuable Curragh handicap early this month; plummeting mark so don't ignore and market worth a look.
runners.comment: Yet to fire for yard; plummeting mark so don't ignore and market worth a look

runners.name: Alphonse Le Grande
runners.spotlight: 2024 Cesarewitch winner was winless in light campaign last year; absent since November and this trip on the short side for seasonal return.
runners.comment: Cesarewitch winner; absent since November and trip on the short side for seasonal return

runners.name: Apercu
runners.spotlight: All five wins over this trip including this race last year (off 88) and Curragh premier handicap month later; out of depth in stakes company afterwards but one to consider back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 5lb lower; respected on return to handicap ranks

races.race_name: Buy Tickets Online And Save Handicap

runners.name: Servare
runners.spotlight: Much-improved on last month's seasonal return when running away with 1m2f Navan handicap off 59 (second has run well since); up 12lb for that and goes further up in trip here in a better race.
runners.comment: Runaway Navan winner up 12lb and goes further up in trip here in a better race

runners.name: Pliny
runners.spotlight: Lots to find with Servare on recent Navan run and one of the more exposed of these, albeit is down to a decent mark.
runners.comment: Lots to find with Servare on recent Navan run and one of the more exposed of these

runners.name: Sea Of Dubka
runners.spotlight: Handicap debut third at Dundalk (10.5f) recently was a definite step in the right direction and likely more to offer back on the turf if staying this longer trip.
runners.comment: Dundalk run encouraging; likely more to offer back on the turf if staying longer trip
runners.owner: Anthony Head & Stephen E McCarthy & Mrs Paul Shanahan

runners.name: Delusional
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on AW at Southwell on second run for Jack Channon; two runs for current yard have been ordinary, beaten 12l by Servare on last month's turf debut, having raced keenly; others preferred.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l by Servare on last month's turf debut, having raced keenly; others preferred

runners.name: Yousaynothingatall
runners.spotlight: Sister to Irish Oaks second Wemighttakedlongway showed improved form on recent return when third in good-class Dundalk fillies' maiden (second has won since); cheekpieces added here for handicap debut off a potentially lenient mark.
runners.comment: Dundalk third reads well; cheekpieces on for handicap debut off a potentially lenient mark

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: Pick of three juvenile efforts when staying-on second in Killarney maiden over a mile in testing conditions; too keen and didn't get home over 1m2f on last month's Navan return so will need to settle better to see out this longer trip and others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Will need to settle better to see out this longer trip and others look better handicapped

runners.name: Nod Of Approval
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens and again on handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m) in October; comeback run here early this month over same trip was disappointing but half-brother to Irish Cesarewitch winner Puturhandstogether a potential improver now upped markedly in distance.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Puturhandstogether a potential improver now upped markedly in distance

runners.name: Darius Dark
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to same yard's 1m6f Listed winner Floresta hasn't shown a whole lot in three qualifying maidens but likely improver stepped up markedly in trip here for handicap debut and yard going well.
runners.comment: Unknown quantity and likely improver upped in trip here for handicap debut

races.race_name: Aguiar Racing Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Master Haku
runners.spotlight: Promising third behind an odds-on shot on debut in a Cork maiden hurdle last November; got closer to the runner-up Grey Jude when beaten a head at the same venue later that month; Grey Jude's official rating of 122 implies that he could be hard to beat here.
runners.comment: Placed form linked to the 122-rated Grey Jude implies that he could be hard to beat here

runners.name: The Store Boy
runners.spotlight: Made promising debut at Tramore last May; has failed to improve but has shown a fair standard of form; not disgraced in handicap company when seventh of 18 in a Leopardstown contest in December; beaten when falling at the last in a rated chase at Fairyhouse in January.
runners.comment: Fell in a rated chase at Fairyhouse in January, definite chance on his best hurdles form

runners.name: Misery Hill
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French AW winner; decent Flat pedigree; Master Haku is the stable's selected
runners.comment: Dam French AW winner, decent Flat pedigree, Master Haku is the stable's selected

runners.name: Endless Talking
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner on good ground for Donnchadh Doyle 12 months ago; mild promise in a Fairyhouse bumper in November; soundly beaten in another bumper at Down Royal in December; fair fourth in an ordinary maiden hurdle at Limerick late last month; better ground should help his cause.
runners.comment: Point winner on good, fair fourth on soft on hurdling debut, drying conditions should help

runners.name: Blues Street
runners.spotlight: E66,000 store purchase as a 3yo; second foal; Blue Bresil gelding out of a Flat/hurdles winner by Jeremy; hooded for debut; trainer saddles two others who have shown ability.
runners.comment: Blue Bresil gelding out of a Flat/hurdles winner, trainer also saddles two with experience

runners.name: Lamikal d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Beat the only other finisher in a maiden point at Borris House last month for Caroline McCaldin; related to French jumps winners; stablemate Endless Talking has better credentials.
runners.comment: Beat the only other finisher in a maiden point, yard's Endless Talking is more appealing

runners.name: Microscope
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in all four points; moderate hurdle/bumper form; remote fifth over 2m6f at Downpatrick last month; unlikely to feature
runners.comment: Pulled up in all four points, moderate hurdle/bumper form, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Betterluckthistime
runners.spotlight: Second foal; by Affinisea out of an unraced half-sister to 2m4f hurdle winner Noble Warrior, out of unraced half-sister Flat/hurdle winner Noble Choice (dam of Galway Plate winner Lord Scoundrel).
runners.comment: By Affinisea out of an unraced half-sister to a hurdles winner, stamina in the pedigree

runners.name: Cullagh Lad
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother by Mahler to 2m maiden hurdle winner Cullaghs Star; dam placed over hurdles; from a solid jumping family.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Mahler to a maiden hurdle winner, from a solid jumping family.

runners.name: Strong Contender
runners.spotlight: Poor last of three finishers in a 3m maiden hurdle at Sligo last October; out of contention when falling two out at Fairyhouse earlier this month.
runners.comment: Poor last of three finishers on debut at Sligo, beaten when falling two out at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Lenko
runners.spotlight: Remote sixth on debut in a Navan race in which his trainer supplied the winner and second; Master Haku represents the stable's best chance here.
runners.comment: Remote sixth on debut at Navan, Master Haku represents the stable's best chance here

runners.name: Lost Features
runners.spotlight: Nothing in her four outings suggests she has any chance in a maiden.
runners.comment: Nothing in her four outings suggests she has any chance in a maiden
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Phoenix Arizona
runners.spotlight: Placed in two small-field maiden hurdles last October; beaten a huge distance when second in a recent point-to-point outing. 90-rated gelding would have a better chance in a handicap
runners.comment: Placed in two small-field maiden hurdles last autumn, very distant second in a point

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in maidens at Punchestown and Cork; three-figure odds on both occasions.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in maidens at Punchestown and Cork, three-figure odds on both occasions

races.race_name: Follow Kilbeggan On Social Media Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Desert Haven
runners.spotlight: Two respectable Flat runs last month; 0-11 over hurdles; comes into the reckoning following an improved display that saw him finish fifth of 21 in a stronger race than this at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Comes into the reckoning following an improved display in a stronger race at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Jerrari
runners.spotlight: Beaten by Malbas over C&D last June before back-to-back wins at Roscommon and Galway, both at around 2m on good ground; testing conditions were against him when pulled up at Navan in February; harder to excuse a poor run in yielding at Downpatrick.
runners.comment: Climbed in the ratings after Roscommon and Galway wins last summer, suited by good ground

runners.name: Kabylia
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on this card last season; placed on all four maiden hurdle starts, two of them at this venue; ground was probably softer than ideal when she ran in a big-field handicap hurdle at Fairyhouse at Easter after a break; has plenty to find with fifth-placed Desert Haven but should be sharper now, and better ground will help.
runners.comment: Bumper winner on this card last year, also placed on both course hurdles starts, respected

runners.name: Survivors Sister
runners.spotlight: Was keeping on when falling at the last in a 2m3f handicap at Limerick in December, went up 11lb for winning a weak 2m5f contest at the same venue in February; struggled at Fairyhouse later that month; not the most reliable sort.
runners.comment: Went up 11lb for a Limerick win, struggled at Fairyhouse, not the most reliable sort
runners.owner: John J McGrath & T C Quinn

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; second of 12 finishers when a beaten favourite at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January; flopped when favourite at Ayr last month; first appearance for Gordon Elliott now
runners.comment: Has joined Gordon Elliott since last month's flop at Ayr, second at Fairyhouse in January

runners.name: Jury Belle
runners.spotlight: Second in a bumper in Britain last summer; had hinted at handicap potential in maidens before a moderate run at Clonmel a month ago.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper in Britain last summer, has hinted at handicap potential in maidens

runners.name: Lady Doyanne
runners.spotlight: Point winner; won a 2m1f Wexford maiden hurdle on rules debut last August; was then poor at Navan on handicap debut; much better but still well beaten at Punchestown on second attempt; first run for six months.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner last August, poor run on handicap debut, well beaten on second try

runners.name: Piccolo Player
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground maiden winner last November; has failed to run to market expectations at Gowran and Limerick on his last two starts; needs to find extra
runners.comment: Has failed to run to market expectations on his last two starts, needs to find extra

runners.name: Malbay Madness
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year and followed up in May; course form is an important asset, so worth considering despite having finished out of the money on his final two starts in 2025; 10lb higher now than when winning snugly 12 months ago on similar ground.
runners.comment: Won this race last year and followed up in May, course form is an important asset here

runners.name: Malbas
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last June, beating today's rival Jerrari; also ran well to take second in a 2m3f contest last August; a recent chase start should have advanced his fitness; cheekpieces added
runners.comment: Beat today's rival Jerrari her last June, has had a recent chase start, cheekpieces added

runners.name: Spinning Web
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner at Thurles last year over 2m5f; not badly treated but probably in need of a confidence boost after two falls over fences; cheekpieces now as well as usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Not badly treated but probably in need of a confidence boost after two falls over fences

runners.name: McLoughlin
runners.spotlight: 80-rated Flat maiden, placed twice in soft-ground maidens last back-end; yet to show an equivalent standard over hurdles;  tongiue-tie on now as well as cheekpieces worn for his best Flat runs; could become competitive on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed twice on Flat, ordinary hurdles form, could become competitive on handicap debut

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: AW winner; fifth behind subsequent chase Grade 1 winner Koktail Divin in a maiden hurdle at Leopardstown (2m4f, yielding); flattered by that detail; pulled up in a Bellewstown maiden hurdle last week; has plenty of weight, hard to fancy.
runners.comment: AW winner, pulled up in maiden hurdle last week, has plenty of weight, hard to fancy

runners.name: Jouster
runners.spotlight: Good form for Gavin Cromwell in the closing months of 2024, featuring a chase win at Tramore and a handicap hurdle win at Fairyhouse; never managed to reach that standard last year.
runners.comment: Good form in the closing months of 2024, never managed to reach that standard last year

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Centaq
runners.spotlight: Best efforts have come over 2m2f at Downpatrick on better ground when runner-up twice in the autumn; ran out at halfway at Down Royal when last seen in December; claims if returning to Downpatrick form
runners.comment: Runner-up twice on better ground at Downpatrick in autumn; claims if recapturing that form

runners.name: Pourquoi Poi
runners.spotlight: Won at Punchestown last autumn (2m4f, good to yielding) off 87 and posted a decent Thurles run next time off current mark (2m4f, yielding to soft); player if ready after absence.
runners.comment: Won on better ground last autumn and decent effort off this mark next time; player

runners.name: Krishna Bellevue
runners.spotlight: Some promise in maidens but beaten 30l on handicap debut here (3m1f, good to yielding) when last seen; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Some promise in maidens but beaten 30l on handicap debut here in Sept'

runners.name: The Toothpicker
runners.spotlight: A couple of encouraging efforts since returning from a long absence, notably when fifth of 24 at Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy); another positive effort when a 7.75l fifth at Limerick latest (2m, soft to heavy); should give a good account once again.
runners.comment: Some encouraging efforts since returning from long absence; fifth at Limerick latest

runners.name: Two Can Plan
runners.spotlight: A first-time tongue tie brought about his best of four maiden hurdle efforts at Leopardstown (2m, soft to heavy) when a 21l sixth; tailed off at Clonmel last time; makes handicap debut now.
runners.comment: 21l sixth was the best of his m'den runs; may do better now in h'caps

runners.name: Mon Sheriffe
runners.spotlight: Third over 2m at Navan in December (2m, soft to heavy) but poor in two subsequent runs on heavy at Naas and Gowran; improved effort at Bellewstown last time when a 9.5l sixth (2m1f, yielding); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Improved effort at Bellewstown last time but others make more appeal
runners.owner: T Leopold, P Smith, D P Connolly & C Connolly

runners.name: The Dancen Kid
runners.spotlight: Went closest when third in a Leopardstown handicap over 2m2f last month on heavy; tame Wexford effort since back at 2m, so needs to bounce back, but this is a lower grade; never raced on ground better than yielding.
runners.comment: Went closest when third in a novice h'cap last month; tame Wexford effort latest

runners.name: Fort Randall
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; beaten 25l when last seen at Down Royal in January but is capable of better for a shrewd yard, so keep an eye on the market.
runners.comment: Reserve; capable of better than 25l defeat last time; check market

runners.name: Ahellofaman
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in last three starts; still early days but hard to fancy on evidence so far.
runners.comment: Tailed off in last three starts; hard to fancy on that basis

runners.name: Ferdia
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in five of six races since returning from over three years off the track; of little appeal.
runners.comment: PU in five of six races since returning from over three years off the track; not appealing

runners.name: Laska Du Breuil
runners.spotlight: First reserve; modest form overall; ninth of 18 at Punchestown in December (2m1f, yielding) but tailed off back there last time; down 2lb and cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Reserve; modest form and tailed off at Punchestown when last seen; down 2lb

runners.name: Sarah's Express
runners.spotlight: Showed bits and pieces of ability in four runs before pulling up on handicap debut at Punchestown (3m, heavy) when found to have bled; 24l fourth in a Clonmel maiden last time (2m3f, soft); more needed.
runners.comment: PU in only h'cap; 24l fourth in a Clonmel m'den last time; more needed

runners.name: Dontdooddson
runners.spotlight: Veteran added a fourth win when landing a Wexford handicap hurdle last summer (2m1f, good); creditable efforts over hurdles and fences over the winter but others preferred.
runners.comment: Veteran continues to show some signs of ability but others preferred
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Coup d'Essay
runners.spotlight: No impact in maidens; beaten 13.5l when eighth if 14 at Limerick on handicap debut last time (2m, soft to heavy); mark looks high at the moment.
runners.comment: No impact in m'dens; beaten 13.5l on h'cap debut last time; mark looks high

runners.name: Dahoma
runners.spotlight: Struggled in maiden hurdles; didn't beat many rivals on handicap debut at Punchestown (2m4f, soft to heavy); bled when pulled up at Leopardstown last time.
runners.comment: Struggled in m'den hurdles; bled when PU at Leopardstown last time for second h'cap

runners.name: Michael's Jet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in completed m'den hurdle runs at big odds; hard to fancy
runners.comment: Tailed off in completed m'den hurdle runs at big odds; hard to fancy

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Bowgate Street
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-24 is immediately off-putting; close third at Galway in October (2m1f, yielding) but overall moderate form last year; 17l eighth of 20 back from a break earlier this month not hugely encouraging.
runners.comment: Record of 0-24 is off-putting; 17l eighth of 20 back from a break not super encouraging

runners.name: Chemdawg
runners.spotlight: Couple of placed efforts in November-December before going with no enthusiasm and being pulled up at Clonmel and Down Royal in opening 2026 runs; back on track with a 6l second at Limerick last time (2m6f, soft); needs to back it up and this is harder.
runners.comment: PU twice this year before bouncing back with a second at Limerick latest; must back it up

runners.name: Jaadil
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the autumn; runner-up at Navan (2m3f, yielding) before gaining a first win in emphatic fashion at Cork (2m3f, good); good effort at Sligo next time off 10lb higher mark when filling the second spot (2m2f, good to yielding); big player and Jack Kennedy is a very interesting booking (1-7 riding for this trainer).
runners.comment: Progressive in the autumn inc' a win; big player and Kennedy is an interesting booking

runners.name: Crafty Gael
runners.spotlight: Winless from 14 but went close on several occasions in 2023; beaten 34l in both starts since returning from 800-day absence; good ground and addition of blinkers need to transform him.
runners.comment: Beaten 34l in both starts since return from 800-day absence

runners.name: Sidiriya
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in 2022; nowhere near as effective a hurdler but penultimate Naas third (2m, soft to heavy) offers a modicum of hope.
runners.comment: Flat winner is nowhere near as effective over hurdles; third at Naas last month gives hope

runners.name: Gino Bella
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner last November and good form since, placing three times; 3.25l fourth at Clonmel earlier this month when last seen (2m, soft); still lightly raced and this ground shouldn't cause problems; claimer takes lessens her burden.
runners.comment: Point winner in Nov' has been running well since; lightly-raced and ground okay

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Not beaten too far in a couple of maidens; not ruled out on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Not beaten too far in a couple of m'dens; not ruled out on h'cap debut

runners.name: Hidalgo Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: 0-20 over hurdles but has placed on several occasions, including at Thurles in November, when last seen over timber (2m, good to yielding); poor run at Dundalk in December; better ground suits and top claimer aboard.
runners.comment: 0-20 over hurdles but good runs in defeat; better ground suits and top claimer aboard

runners.name: Jinxs Link
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins include a success here; tailed off in a Fairyhouse beginners' chase returning from almost a year off and pulled up over hurdles at the same venue last time; needs to return to form.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins inc' here; poor in two starts since return from almost a year off

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: Lost form after winning at Clonmel in December (2m1f, heavy); caught the attention of the stewards when a never-nearer fifth back at Clonmel earlier this month (2m1f, yielding); never dangerous over fences at Tramore five days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Lost form after winning at Clonmel in Dec'; positives to take from recent run back there

runners.name: Embrace The Day
runners.spotlight: Promise when winning a point-to-point for Jonathan Fogarty but didn't show much in three maiden hurdles nor at Fairyhouse (2m4f, heavy) on handicap debut when ignored in the market; never dangerous at Clonmel last time (2m3f, soft); perhaps a tongue tie can get more from him.
runners.comment: Point winner hasn't shown much under rules' inc' in two h'cap runs

runners.name: Thurman
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form; best watched for first handicap run.
runners.comment: Modest maiden form; best watched for first handicap run

runners.name: Thatsmyroadie
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and tailed off in completed maiden hurdle starts; vast improvement required on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Huge odds and tailed off in completed m'den hurdles; vast improvement needed

runners.name: Live My Dream
runners.spotlight: Beaten 20l on handicap debut at Clonmel last month (2m3f, soft); improvement needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l on handicap debut at Clonmel last month (2m3f, soft); improvement needed

runners.name: Crab Road
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maiden hurdles and beaten 53l on Flat debut at Dundalk last time; passed over on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Poor form in m'den hurdles and beaten 53l on Flat last time; passed over on h'cap debut

runners.name: Loughrask Jet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on handicap debut at Thurles (2m4f, heavy); better ground needs to bring improvement.
runners.comment: Tailed off on handicap debut; better ground needs to bring improvement

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Live To Laugh
runners.spotlight: In great form at the moment over fences; followed up a wide-margin Thurles success (2m6f, soft) when beating Plain Or Battered by 2l at Clonmel next time (3m, soft); runs off 18lb lower hurdles mark; big player.
runners.comment: In great form over fences, winning last twice; runs off 18lb lower hurdle mark; big player
runners.owner: J Whittle/M McEvoy/Good For A Laugh Synd'/A Callan

runners.name: Coppola
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in all three starts since joining this yard over trips of an extended 2m4f up to 3m at Down Royal and Downpatrick; cheekpieces go on now; in the mix once again.
runners.comment: Runner-up in all three starts for this yard; in the mix once again

runners.name: Dolly Watkins
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with an improved display at Limerick; made an encouraging if belated return when second at Down Royal last month; had plenty to spare when winning at Limerick a fortnight later in a mares-only contest; up 7lb and this looks harder.
runners.comment: Plenty to spare when winning a mares-only contest at Limerick; up 7lb and this is harder

runners.name: Sean Mc
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Wexford earlier this month (2m4f, good to yielding) when keeping on well; up 6lb but that was his first time running on better ground and there could be more improvement to come now trying this trip for the first time.
runners.comment: Up 6lb after Wexford win earlier this month; could be more improvement in him.

runners.name: Plain Or Battered
runners.spotlight: In good form under both codes recently; 3lb outside the weights at Leopardstown but took the third spot (3m, soft); backed that up when runner-up in a Clonmel novice handicap chase last month (3m, soft); withdrawn due to unsuitable good ground at Down Royal last time, so conditions a question.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles and fences recently; ground conditions may not suit
runners.owner: David Norris & Fergal Norris & N Dooly

runners.name: Courageous Cleo
runners.spotlight: Made little impact in three maiden hurdle outings; respect for the stable is the only positive factor, but Kennedy rides Coppola.
runners.comment: Little impact in three m'den hurdles; Kennedy rides Coppola.

runners.name: Creadan Grace
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; upped to 3m for a 20-runner race at Down Royal in October (good to yielding) and it brought improvement with a last-gasp win; another nice effort at Ascot (3m1f, good to soft) off higher British mark when a 3.5l third; may need this first run back but ground to suit.
runners.comment: Upped to 3m to win at Down Royal and ran well at Ascot when last seen; returns from break

runners.name: La Tene
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward in a 2m3f handicap hurdle at Clonmel (soft) on latest, outpaced and rallying for a 7l third; way below that level over fences at Tramore last time (2m, yielding).
runners.comment: Good third at Clonmel but way below that level over fences at Tramore last time
runners.owner: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan

runners.name: Dalmatic
runners.spotlight: Battled to gain a first win when successful over hurdles at Limerick in December off 84 (2m6f, soft to heavy); tailed off at Punchestown next time; bounced back when runner-up back at Limerick last time (2m7f, heavy), had Mickey Hulie 17l behind in third that day; best form on softer.
runners.comment: Battled to gain first win in December; bounced back from poor effort at Limerick last time

runners.name: Mickey Hulie
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; confirmed his liking for a stamina test when second over 3m at Naas (soft to heavy) last month; below that level when a remote third of 15 at Limerick (2m7f, heavy; favourite) eight days later; perhaps that came too soon; cheekpieces go on now and better ground should be okay based on his point-to-point wins.
runners.comment: Dual point winner was a neck second at Naas last month; better ground should be okay

runners.name: Ardera Gun
runners.spotlight: Best placing came at Down Royal last time when seventh of 19 (3m, good), still finished 24l behind all the same; others preferred.
runners.comment: Best placing came last time when seventh but still beaten 24l; others preferred

runners.name: Anodandawink
runners.spotlight: Out of his depth in maidens, always at big odds; finished second in a point-to-point when with Derek O'Connor; inauspicious start to handicaps when beaten 22l at Clonmel last time; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Out of depth in m'dens and moderate h'cap debut; cheekpieces on now

runners.name: Mistyburn
runners.spotlight: Point winner is yet to score on the track but ran well enough in a 3m Cork handicap hurdle last time (yielding); down 2lb and the tongue tie is left off now; O'Keeffe takes over from claimer now.
runners.comment: Point winner is yet to score on the track; ran well enough at Cork last time when third

runners.name: Midleton Rare
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner this season, the latest coming at Bellewstown last time off 6lb lower (3m, yielding); in and out form, so not certain to back up his win.
runners.comment: Three-time winner this season inc' last time; up 6lb and not certain to back it up

runners.name: Mr Dibbs
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner here in 2024 (2m4f, good); good second at Sligo when last seen under rules' in August last year off 97 (2m5f, good) in final start for Adrian Murray; point-to-point form since then raises questions
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner here in 2024; recent point form raises questions

runners.name: It's Time Again
runners.spotlight: In good form in 2024-2025; placed at Limerick and Gowran before adding a third hurdling win at Ballinrobe (2m6f, good), all three wins coming at that venue; unseen since that success just over a year ago; check market.
runners.comment: Unseen since adding third hurdles win at Ballinrobe in April last year; check market

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Beginners Chase

runners.name: Gaoth Chuil
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner with some excellent efforts in competitive handicaps; second behind Perceval Legallois at DRF last season off 133; ran to a good level on chase debut over C&D a year ago; failed to repeat that until last month's Cork second, confirming winning potential
runners.comment: Second in a C&D mares' chase a year ago, Cork second last month confirms winning potential

runners.name: Give Him A Chance
runners.spotlight: Course winner over hurdles (2m4f good) last summer; gained his third hurdles win in February; mishap at Wexford last time, when slipping up early.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles, gained his third hurdles win in February, mishap last time
runners.owner: John T O'Connor & Michael P Gleeson

runners.name: My Trump Card
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner has been disappointing in six chase starts, jumping poorly; well held by Pray Tell on Punchestrown running.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdles winner, disappointing in six chase starts, jumping is a source of concern

runners.name: Pray Tell
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 3 novice hurdle (3m) at Cork in December 2024; made a bright start over fences when second to Argento Boy at Punchestown; fell five out in the race won by Solder In Milan at the same venue; kept ticking over with a hurdles run.
runners.comment: Grade 3 novice hurdle winner in 2024, ran well on his only completed start over fences

runners.name: Supersundae
runners.spotlight: Rated 142 over hurdles; seventh in the 2024 Supreme Novices' Hurdle; 8l second behind The Yellow Clay in a Grade 1 novice hurdle at Naas in January 2025 (2m4f, soft); pulled up in two following hurdle starts at Grade 1 level;  form of his two chase starts bears little relation to his best hurdles form.
runners.comment: 142-rated hurdle, form of his two chase starts bears little relation to that standard

runners.name: Billie Frechette
runners.spotlight: Useful form on the Flat and over hurdles (rated 120); suited by staying trips on the Flat; should be fit for chasing debut after AW run at the beginning of the month.
runners.comment: Useful form on the Flat and over hurdles, should be fit for chasing debut after AW run

runners.name: Rue Taylor
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles at Wexford, notably final start last term in 3m novice in April (yielding), relishing stamina test; no sign of her best hurdles form on last three runs.
runners.comment: Has won twice over hurdles at Wexford, no sign of her best hurdles form on last three runs

runners.name: Maxi Mac Gold
runners.spotlight: 126-rated hurdler; not as effective over fences; best effort when second over 3m2f at Fairyhouse in February; pulled up in a competitive handicap last time; needs to improve.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a competitive handicap since a good Fairyhouse run, needs to improve

runners.name: The Burrow
runners.spotlight: Point winner; ordinary form over hurdles, well below the level of many of these rivals; tailed off on chasing debut at Cork.
runners.comment: Point winner, ordinary form over hurdles, tailed off on chasing debut at Cork

runners.name: Astley
runners.spotlight: A distant runner-up in a bumper at Clonmel (2m4f, good to yielding); beat very few rivals in three runs over hurdles; started over fences with a respectable run at Down Royal recently.
runners.comment: Weak hurdles form, started over fences with a respectable run at Down Royal recently

runners.name: Bobbysingindablues
runners.spotlight: Minor placed form over hurdles in 2024; nothing of much note in his form since then; readily discounted.
runners.comment: Minor placed form over hurdles in 2024, nothing of much note in his form since then

runners.name: Gatsby Grey
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler; nothing approaching that level over fences; pulled up on last two starts; has burst blood vessels.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler, nothing approaching that level over fences, pulled up on last two starts

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Handicap Chase

runners.name: Iamagetaway
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner over 2m4f on good; best effort over fences when second in a 2m5f Roscommon beginners' event last June, form shown in a good light by the progressive winner Boston Rover; failed to build on that in two subsequent novice runs; weak form in two handicaps last autumn; makes debut for top stable after a break
runners.comment: Initial promise over fences, weak form in handicaps, debut for top stable after a break

runners.name: Stoneyford Lady
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler; second in a Thurles beginners' event before making going one better in a handicap at the same venue (2m2f, yielding); failed to recover from a bad mistake two out at Limerick after Christmas.
runners.comment: Won at Thurles on her second chase start, bad mistake two out at Limerick was costly

runners.name: Hitthehayson
runners.spotlight: Winner of both starts over fences; 15lb higher than when beating Duffys Hodey over 3m at Fairyhouse in November, having backed that up with a 2m4f win at Haydock; placed off his much lower hurdles mark in two outings this month.
runners.comment: Winner of both starts over fences, 15lb higher than when beating Duffy's Hodey in November

runners.name: Favori Logique
runners.spotlight: Two handicap chase wins, including over C&D in July 2024; fine comeback run after 16 months' absence at Leopardstown when a staying-on third behind Harbour Highway over 2m6f (yielding); ground may been too testing at Limerick more recently.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024, good run on his penultimate start, stable in good form

runners.name: Tell Nobody Nothin
runners.spotlight: Now 0-10 over fences having been an unplaced favourite over a longer trip at Down Royal 12 days ago; this is probably closer to his optimum distance; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, now 0-10 over fences, unplaced favourite at Down Royal 12 days ago

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 16 starts in points; recent last of eight finishers at Down Royal provides no encouragement.
runners.comment: Only one win from 16 starts in points, recent run at Down Royal provides no encouragement

runners.name: Justabitofyours
runners.spotlight: Looks harshly treated; struggled in beginners' events; never in contention and pulled up at Fairyhouse on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Looks harshly treated, never in contention and pulled up at Fairyhouse on handicap debut

runners.name: Ballybrittas
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer and hurdler; runner-up on first two attempts chasing, in beginners' at Thurles (2m5f) and Clonmel (2m1f) last season; has failed to build on those promising efforts, stablemate Duffys Hodey is more appealing currently.
runners.comment: Runner-up on his first two chase starts, has failed to build on those promising efforts

runners.name: Harbour Highway
runners.spotlight: Weak form over fences until coming good at Leopardstown early this month, with the returning Favori Logique in third; something amiss at Fairyhouse last time, never in contention and pulled up; 5lb higher than for Leopardstown win; could bounce back to form; 7lb claimed.
runners.comment: Something amiss at Fairyhouse last time, 5lb higher than for Leopardstown win, 7lb off

runners.name: Riyami
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner including in 2m event at Dundalk on penultimate start; in rear in a 2m Lingfield race three weeks ago; has won over hurdles; makes chase debut
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner including on penultimate start, has won over hurdles, chase debut

runners.name: Quornofamonday
runners.spotlight: Gained a second chase success with a good display over 2m2f at Thurles in February; pulled up at the same venue on next start; jockey lost an iron at an early fence at Fairyhouse, and was unseated four out; blinkered for the first time now.
runners.comment: Two failures to complete since a Thurles win in February, blinkered now, others preferred

runners.name: Good World
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdles winner; limited chasing experience and lacks a recent outing; however, potentially well handicapped, 8lb below the current hurdles rating he earned with a Fairyhouse win last November.
runners.comment: Five-time hurdles winner, limited chasing experience but potentially well handicapped

runners.name: The Blaggard
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden hurdle winner in 2024; has failed to make his presence felt in handicap hurdles; no chasing experience.
runners.comment: Tramore maiden hurdle winner in 2024, has failed to make his presence felt in handicaps

runners.name: Musta Lovea Lovea
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 22 starts under rules; struggled in two chase starts early last year; faces a huge task after a long break.
runners.comment: Still a maiden after 22 starts under rules, struggled in two chase starts early last year

races.race_name: Racing Again On Friday 15th May INH Flat Race

runners.name: Streamhill
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase was never a factor in two points but returned from lengthy absence to finish fourth of 13 to Willie Mullins-trained favourite in Cork bumper (yielding) last month; stable 4-11 (plus two seconds) in bumpers this season; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Returned from absence to finish fourth at Cork; stable 4-11 in bumpers this season

runners.name: Seven Towers
runners.spotlight: Promising second run in points; travelled well for a long way when beaten 2.5l into third in Fairyhouse bumper (the winner ran well in Champion Bumper); never travelled a yard in testing conditions at Thurles; more suitable conditions here.
runners.comment: Player judged on penultimate run; completely amiss last time but was in testing conditions

runners.name: Carrigeen Kirkia
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam point winner/placed in 2m2f-3m chases, sister to Irish Grand National winner Rogue Angel, half-sister to useful bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Fire In His Eyes; never figured in five point runs; hooded.
runners.comment: Dam point winner, sister to Irish Grand National winner; 0-5 in points

runners.name: Bells Grove
runners.spotlight: Placed on all three starts in bumpers, on latest in October when third at Cheltenham on final start for John McConnell; market can reveal expectations on first run for new yard with 0-9 record in bumpers.
runners.comment: Third at Cheltenham on final start for John McConnell; market can reveal expectations

runners.name: Mila Ballerina
runners.spotlight: Australia mare; closely related to 1m2f-1m4f Flat and high-class 2m hurdle winner Jason The Militant, half-sister to 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Will Carver; dam 2m hurdle winner; yard's first bumper runner of the season is market noted.
runners.comment: Closely related to the classy Jason The Militant; yard's first bumper runner of the season

runners.name: Pets Georgina
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred but was beaten 46l on bumper debut at Limerick when sent off at 40-1; may need more time.
runners.comment: Nicely bred but was beaten 46l on bumper debut at Limerick when sent off at 40-1

runners.name: Lultimatom
runners.spotlight: Won point by 8l last March and subsequently sold for £225,000; fifth of 14 at 11-4 on bumper debut at Fairyhouse (yielding to soft) in November (Grade 2 winner Moonverrin in second); turned over at odds-on by Dan Skelton-trained runner in Fakenham bumper in January but sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Easy point winner; fifth on bumper debut; turned over at odds-on at Fakenham; in the mix

runners.name: Double Indian
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to six winners at trips ranging from 7f-1m4f; dam unplaced 8.6f-1m4f, half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Excellent Result; Flat-bred and would only be of interest if the market spoke in his favour.
runners.comment: Flat-bred and unlikely to make an impact in this sphere; best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Poets Rambler
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Little Actress; dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f/2m Flat and high-class 2m-2m3f hurdle winner Iktitaf, out of 6f winning sister to US 8.5f Grade 3 winner; stable 25 per cent in bumpers last five seasons; watch betting.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Little Actress; stable 25% in bumpers last five seasons

runners.name: Its My Twist
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; half-brother to bumper winner Major Robinson; unraced dam out of half-sister to Champion Hurdle winners Morley Street and Granville Again; yard 0-10 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Unraced dam out of half-sister to two Champion Hurdle winners; reserve

runners.name: Greenhills Of Cork
runners.spotlight: Made a pleasing debut in Listowel bumper (good to yielding) in September, beaten 4.5l into fourth of 18 (form worked out nicely); well below that level on soft at Thurles in February; solid claims back on more suitable ground.
runners.comment: Fine effort on debut at Listowel on similar ground; didn't enjoy soft last time; player

runners.name: Majestic Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; closely related to winners One Big Bang (useful 2m7f-3m hurdle/chase) and Cool Park (2m4f hurdle), half-brother to three winners; dam maiden half-sister to high-class chaser Madison Du Berlais; yard 2-67 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Tenth foal; closely related to winners One Big Bang and Cool Park; yard 2-67 in bumpers

runners.name: It's All Clear
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when sent off at big prices at Naas and Clonmel; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Well beaten when sent off at big prices at Naas and Clonmel; major improvement required
runners.owner: Paul M O'Loughlin & W D Gannon

runners.name: Jodoro
runners.spotlight: Some promise on last of three bumper runs last year when fifth of 16 at Navan (yielding); 150-1 and pulled up on hurdles debut at Down Royal; others preferred.
runners.comment: Some promise on final bumper run last year but pulled up on hurdles bow; others preferred

runners.name: Kershaw
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to a subsequent rules winner in point last spring; shaped quite well behind the subsequent Champion Bumper fourth at Down Royal in December; should be up to winning a race but this is a competitive one; yard 0-18 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well behind the subsequent Champion Bumper fourth at Down Royal in December

races.race_name: Dunraven Windows Miss Millbrook Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Quay Item
runners.spotlight: Scored comfortably at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) on second attempt under rules; outstayed by the winner, who is useful, over 3m at Doncaster since; may still have more to offer and holds major claims back down in trip.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Plumpton; outstayed over 3m since; major player back down in trip
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & M & L Sainsbury

runners.name: Treasure Planet
runners.spotlight: Juvenile hurdler whose reasonably consistent form features an autumn win; keeping-on third upped to 2m3f in Taunton handicap (good) most recently; may show progress kept to this distance; respected.
runners.comment: Juvenile hurdler who kept on for third upped to 2m3f last time; may show progress

runners.name: Beau Quali
runners.spotlight: Chase winner for previous yard; 0-13 over hurdles but is in form for new stable, having finished second in handicaps on last two outings, latest over C&D (good to soft) behind easy winner; fighting chance at the weights.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles but is in form, runner-up over C&D last time; fighting chance

runners.name: Piping Rock
runners.spotlight: Weakened into third at Plumpton (2m4f) last time but still has consistent overall form figures, which now read 32333, and subsequent physical tweak (wind surgery) may enable him to finish better; solid chance.
runners.comment: Solid contender who has consistent form figures; had wind surgery since last run

runners.name: The Last Promise
runners.spotlight: Trailed home last of seven in Uttoxeter bumper for an inauspicious debut; needs a huge step forward.
runners.comment: Trailed home last of seven in Uttoxeter bumper
runners.owner: Mrs P And Miss S Bailey

runners.name: Equity Release
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on form; beaten long way at Chepstow (2m7f) on stable/hurdles debut most recently.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

runners.name: Drumlee Orders
runners.spotlight: Beaten long way over 2m5f at Ludlow, failing to build on his Irish point effort; chance partly depends on how well he responds to fitting of tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance partly depends on how well he responds to fitting of tongue-tie

runners.name: Yellow Warning
runners.spotlight: Not a particularly solid option, with his two completions comprising an Irish point success and heavy defeat in latest hurdles attempt.
runners.comment: His completions comprise a point win and heavy defeat
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Shuil Rocco
runners.spotlight: Holds poor claims on his two rules efforts and is likely one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Poor claims on his two rules efforts

races.race_name: Sun Trade Windows Final Pride Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loup De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Not an unlucky loser, but may have run Broomfields Cave closer at Wincanton latest without pecking two out (3m1f, good); respected now back on track, though notably remains winless around a galloping track.
runners.comment: 5l behind Broomfields Cave latest (3m1f); all wins gained at sharper tracks than Chepstow.

runners.name: Inflexible
runners.spotlight: Right back to form on last month's second start after 16 months out, just holding on in a Uttoxeter Class 4 (3m, good to soft); form of that win remains untested, but 4lb rise probably fair; effective on good also.
runners.comment: Just held on to land Uttoxeter Class 4 latest (good to soft); handles sound surface also.
runners.owner: H Stephen Smith & The Gabbertas Family

runners.name: Broomfields Cave
runners.spotlight: Chasing record reads 3-6 following this month's 5l defeat of Loup De Maulde at Wincanton (3m1f, good; RPR 128), and although taking him to a new career-high mark the 4lb rise for that doesn't look an overreaction; no track or going issues, and case looks solid once more.
runners.comment: Can uphold Wincanton form over Loup De Maulde despite rise to a new career-high mark.

runners.name: Charles Ritz
runners.spotlight: Last month's near-5l third at Kempton was his most convincing effort to date both over 3m and on good ground, but also took his record in handicaps off marks in the 120s to 0-8; still needs to find a bit more.
runners.comment: Saw out 3m better last time (good), but defeats off marks in the 120s keep adding up.

runners.name: Amateur
runners.spotlight: Last four victories all gained over Ffos Las's 3m4f trip in April or May (good, good to soft); present attainment level is conjectural, having survived just two fences on his recent first outing since last May.
runners.comment: This is his time of year, but was an early casualty on recent first outing since May 2025.

runners.name: Manothepeople
runners.spotlight: Chasing career highlights include a C&D novice handicap and the same Cheltenham April 3m2f event two years running, all on good; 3lb below his last winning mark, but has a listless recent return run to forgive.
runners.comment: Trip, ground and mark are all fine, but has a listless recent return run to forgive.

races.race_name: Braceys My Coranna Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: My Boy Aaron
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but placed over hurdles on his last three starts (2m, soft/good to soft) and that puts him high on the list in a race that might not take much winning; ran well on good ground in a bumper.
runners.comment: 0-8 but placed on last three starts and he's high on the list in this modest contest

runners.name: Mariole
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten sixth of 12 at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month and he needs a bigger performance today; however, he showed some promise when third of eight on hurdle debut at Taunton (2m3f, heavy), when fading late on, and he could come good for his top yard at some point.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten last time but could come good for his top yard at some point

runners.name: Polemon
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner who has also shown promise over hurdles; well beaten in this code last May and bought out of Denis Hogan's yard for £17,000 later that month; absent since but he's one of the likelier winners if at the top of his game on this comeback and stable debut; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Makes stable debut after absence but one of the likelier winners judged on Irish form

runners.name: Both Of Us
runners.spotlight: Progressive on the Flat last year; tailed off on hurdle debut at Stratford last month before 32l third over this C&D (good to soft) three weeks ago, behind two useful sorts; this race should take a lot less winning.
runners.comment: 32l third behind two useful sorts over C&D on second hurdle start; this is weaker
runners.owner: Mrs H Watson & Mrs Carol Lee

runners.name: Queens Up
runners.spotlight: Third in a point (3m, yielding to soft) in December on her second start; however, pulled up at 33-1 on hurdle debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) ten days ago and needs to be an entirely different proposition today.
runners.comment: Third in a point in December but pulled up at 33-1 on recent hurdle debut at Newton Abbot

runners.name: Good Girl Rachel
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability when third in a point in January but tailed off subsequently on her first two runs over hurdles.
runners.comment: Third in a point in January but tailed off subsequently on her first two runs over hurdles

runners.name: Trapper John
runners.spotlight: Fairly encouraging fifth of 18 on hurdle debut at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) in December; a lesser run followed at Lingfield (2m, soft) but he's since gone close on the Flat at Kempton (2m, AW), in February; also effective on good ground on the Flat; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Encouraging run on the first of his two hurdle starts; went close on the Flat last time
runners.owner: Andrew McGladdery And Park Lodge Stables

runners.name: Whatcombe
runners.spotlight: After showing promise he became disappointing during his short Flat career, and he was beaten 19l when fourth of six on his recent hurdle debut at Fakenham (2m, good); however, that was his first run since last November and it's possible he'll get closer to the target today.
runners.comment: Became disappointing on the Flat and no threat on h'dle debut, but could build on that run

runners.name: Jubertilee
runners.spotlight: 6-21 in points and in good heart in that sphere of late; makes rules debut at age of nine but in a race lacking depth; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: 6-21 in points; makes rules debut at age of nine but in a race lacking depth

runners.name: Grand Harbour
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden on the Flat and soundly beaten at 100-1 on last month's hurdle debut at Taunton (2m, good); improvement is needed.
runners.comment: Modest maiden on Flat and soundly beaten at 100-1 on last month's hurdle debut at Taunton

runners.name: Go Try
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the useful Perfect Myth but he was tailed off at 100-1 on last month's belated debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good).
runners.comment: Half-brother to useful Perfect Myth but tailed off at 100-1 on last month's belated debut

runners.name: Caspada
runners.spotlight: Ran okay when fourth of six on debut in bumper at Sedgefield in December but well beaten on his three runs since, the last two being over hurdles.
runners.comment: Ran okay on debut in bumper at Sedgefield but well beaten on his three runs since

runners.name: Lakeview Lass
runners.spotlight: Related to winners but tailed off in both bumpers and same outcome when 125-1 for recent hurdle debut over C&D (good to soft).
runners.comment: Tailed off in both bumpers and same outcome when 125-1 for recent hurdle debut over C&D

runners.name: Angel Wave
runners.spotlight: His dam is from a useful family but he was tailed off on his first two starts and an early faller at Ffos Las 12 days ago.
runners.comment: His dam is from a useful family but he was tailed off on his two completed starts

races.race_name: Braceys My Coranna Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Miss Kingston
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent at big prices in four Irish hurdling starts but her fourth of 12 at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January sets the standard; secured for £22,000 a fortnight later; also ran creditably on better ground in November and she's much respected on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on last of four starts in Ireland; much respected on stable debut

runners.name: One Island
runners.spotlight: Won a Flat race for newcomers at Le Mans (1m3f, good to soft) in May on his only start in France; dropped out tamely to be well beaten on hurdling debut at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft; 11-4) in November and it was a weakening third at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) in January; the pair who finished in front of him that day are decent sorts, however, and maybe the tongue-tie will help; considered.
runners.comment: Has been weakening but tongue-tie may help and the pair that beat him last time are useful

runners.name: Cheap Sandwiches
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability on bumper debut at Warwick (2m, good to soft) in December but disappointed in two subsequent starts; has winning hurdlers in the family so may get back on track switched to this sphere.
runners.comment: Ability on bumper debut but disappointed after; may get back on track switched to hurdles

runners.name: Maestrow
runners.spotlight: Started life in a Listed bumper but that run and the two that followed it, including over hurdles three weeks ago, suggest he'll be more at home in modest handicaps.
runners.comment: Two bumpers and a hurdles run suggest he'll be more at home in modest handicaps.

runners.name: Louis Des Landes
runners.spotlight: Hint of ability on February's hurdling debut and he didn't go unbacked before a bad mistake and unseating early at Wincanton 12 days ago; market might help.
runners.comment: Didn't go unbacked before bad mistake and unseat early 12 days ago; market might help

runners.name: Nowherenearmilan
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on second bumper start and she plugged on for a very modest third at Stratford (good; first-time cheekpieces) on hurdling debut last month; again outpaced when fifth over C&D (also 2m, good to soft) a fortnight ago; cheekpieces removed for this third go over hurdles and while not dismissed she'll likely do better up in trip once handicapping.
runners.comment: Might be one for further once handicapping but he's not entirely ruled out

runners.name: Aimeric
runners.spotlight: Really smart Flat campaigner although he was susceptible to the odd aberration; in fine form as recently as last April, when just denied in valuable 1m6f Musselburgh handicap, but last two starts for Karl Burke showed the other side of his coin and left that yard for 21,000gns in November; still rates a fascinating hurdles debutant with the market potentially offering significant clues.
runners.comment: Smart Flat horse but well beaten last two starts; fascinating runner on stable/hdles debut

runners.name: Just Call Me Buzz
runners.spotlight: 9-1 and outpaced when fifth of seven on bumper debut at Exeter (good) last month; pedigree suggests he'll do better over jumps but probably over further.
runners.comment: Outpaced in one bumper; pedigree suggests he'll do better over jumps; hurdles debut

runners.name: Footage Detectives
runners.spotlight: Big defeats in a bumper and two novices; probably more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Big defeats in a bumper and two novices; probably more one for handicaps

runners.name: Teletime
runners.spotlight: Dam was prolific across both codes and disciplines; looked to need the experience when midfield on bumper debut at Taunton (good to soft) in February; may do better on this first go over hurdles.
runners.comment: Looked to need the experience on bumper debut; may do better now switched to hurdles

runners.name: Five Valleys
runners.spotlight: No immediate promise when 22-1 and weakening quickly to beat one home in October Worcester bumper; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: No immediate promise when 22-1 and weakening to beat one home in October bumper

runners.name: Scrumpyjack
runners.spotlight: Can't be recommended on his outings in a bumper or maiden and novice; has twice been handy before weakening so wind surgery might have helped.
runners.comment: Can't be recommended on his outings in a bumper or maiden and novice; wind surgery

races.race_name: Dunraven Windows Gats And Co Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Torneo
runners.spotlight: Jumping let him down in what was effectively a 0-150 handicap here latest (2m3f, good to soft); his string of low-130s RPRs before that read well in tonight's company, however, and although still 9lb above any mark defied he did land a C&D novice handicap last year on one other visit.
