runners.comment: Consistent before finding a Class 2 over further too big an ask here latest; player.

runners.name: Bonza Boy
runners.spotlight: Sometime tendency to jump right hasn't been sufficient to deny him victory around here in the past, and he's never hit the deck in 22 career starts; issue is more that he's now 0-10 racing from official marks above the 104 he denied at Exeter in January (2m1f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Exeter and Chepstow winner with cut; plenty of chances off tonight's sort of mark.

runners.name: Hypotenus
runners.spotlight: Able to dominate when cashing in a career-low mark at Taunton in January (2m, good), his sole win in 14 chasing starts; hasn't gone on since, but back to within 2lb of that figure now and one previous wind operation provoked a small spike in form; chance on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Back to within 2lb of the mark he lowered at Taunton in January (2m, good); stable debut.

runners.name: Parramount
runners.spotlight: Chance on November's Doncaster hurdles second, less so on all which has followed; unexposed in this sphere, with one start over fences compared to the other trio's 41 combined, but none too convincing on that belated chase debut at Wetherby latest (1m7f, good), jumping left and weakening.
runners.comment: Struggling for form since November's hurdles second; unconvincing chase debut latest.

races.race_name: Sun Trade Windows Cherry Gold Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: American Land
runners.spotlight: Taunton win this time last year was the first leg of an eventual hat-trick (2m1f-2m3f) all recorded on good or quicker surfaces, so drying forecast is all to the good; chance, assuming he's not goaded into making the same sorts of errors as on his recent return here.
runners.comment: Pestered in lead and made errors on recent return; would certainly appreciate a dryout.

runners.name: The Kemble Brewery
runners.spotlight: Endured a sobering chase debut here in November (sole start on good) but firmly back on track on sole start since when finding only an even less exposed handicapper too good over C&D a fortnight ago (good to soft; pair 9l clear); cheekpieces added; retains plenty of upside.
runners.comment: Bumped into an even less exposed handicapper over C&D latest (pair 9l clear); player.

runners.name: Inside Man
runners.spotlight: Never able to get competitive returning from five months out in a big-field Sussex Champion Hurdle recently, but September's handicap debut win came off only 2lb lower than today's mark and he landed a good-ground C&D novice event exactly a year ago; could well get back on track.
runners.comment: C&D novice winner this time last year; handicap win came off just 2lb lower; chance.

runners.name: Razzle Dazzle Boy
runners.spotlight: C&D seconds either side of January's Leicester win (1m7f, heavy) speak well of his effectiveness around galloping tracks with at least some give underfoot; drying forecast worries more than latest 1lb rise.
runners.comment: Leicester win and two C&D seconds since Christmas all gained with some give underfoot.

runners.name: Wreckless Eric
runners.spotlight: For two years in a row now he's followed a second in the Imperial Cup with a disappointing effort at Aintree, second-time cheekpieces presumably not to blame latest as they're retained here; record of 1F12 in handicaps of Class 3 or below, and ought not mind any dryout (2-4 on good).
runners.comment: Aintree flop latest isn't without precedent; easier task here and ought not mind a dryout.

races.race_name: Dunraven Bowl Pink Eyed Pedro "Grassroots' Open Hunters" Chase

runners.name: Stumps Or Slips
runners.spotlight: Rampant in sub-3m good-ground hurdle races and chases last summer, and has the beating of Yippee Ki Yay on a line through Macklin, whom he trounced at Exeter last time (3m, good); still fairly lightly raced over this sort of trip, and drying forecast rates a big plus.
runners.comment: Proved stamina for 3m with ease at Exeter latest (good) and still on the up; big player.

runners.name: Yippee Ki Yay
runners.spotlight: Track and going types come alike to this former Kelso and Exeter hunter chase winner (soft and good to soft); 11l behind a rival later drubbed by Stumps Or Slips on his most recent hunter chase start, but confidence levels topped up with an easy point win at Eyton-on-Severn (good to firm) since.
runners.comment: Appears to have something to find with Stumps Or Slips, but no track or going type issues.
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs Ben Herbert And Family

runners.name: Windance
runners.spotlight: Landed back-to-back Fontwell and Wincanton staying handicap chases three years ago (good to soft); blameless exit behind Stumps Or Slips latest (hampered) but capable of throwing in his own errors or jumping right; form of this year's completed efforts falls well short of the required.
runners.comment: Blameless exit behind Stumps Or Slips latest, but not in the form this year to figure.

runners.name: Important Notice
runners.spotlight: Remains the last horse to beat hunter chase star Barton Snow, albeit back in late 2024 on desperate ground; twice a Trebudannon open point winner (good to soft) since January's Hereford hunter chase exit which was not of his own making, but an unseat at Stratford last Sunday is harder to forgive.
runners.comment: Late-2024 defeat of Barton Snow looks better form now; unseated at Stratford last Sunday.

runners.name: Pink Eyed Pedro
runners.spotlight: Splendid old-timer who has won both confined novice (2019) and grassroots (2025) incarnations of this race (each on good); always held by a match-fit and five-years younger rival on this month's seasonal return, albeit beaten just 2l; likely to be spot on for his title defence.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025, six years after first taking the same prize; 2l second on recent return.

runners.name: Poppa Poutine
runners.spotlight: Hampered when unseating in an open point at neighbouring Howick last month, and generally a safe ride; both handicap chase wins (from 17 starts over fences) have come over Wincanton's 3m1f on ground softer than good, and the drying forecast isn't strictly ideal.
runners.comment: Hampered when unseating latest; experienced chaser, with all wins on softer than good.

races.race_name: Braceys Master Charm Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Star Walking
runners.spotlight: Effectively a 0-135 mares' handicap she took in course record time at Kempton over Christmas (good); can throw in the odd poor effort and there's something to take on trust after she stopped early on latest start (made a noise) but she's in the mix if all is well with her.
runners.comment: Stopped early latest, but broke course record for Kempton's 3m over Christmas (good).

runners.name: Roaring Home
runners.spotlight: Below par on two of her last three outings, including latest when returned to the sound surface of her one career success; hasn't shaped as if in need of this second crack at a stayers' trip (pulled up in the first).
runners.comment: Below par on two of her last three outings, including latest; trip is not an obvious plus.

runners.name: Love Me
runners.spotlight: Big-field Navan winner in autumn 2024 and best British efforts have similarly been recorded in handicap hurdles around galloping tracks; trip didn't beat her when upped to this C&D latest (good to soft), failing by just over 2l, and she's not wholly dismissed despite the hotter company.
runners.comment: Seemed to pass stamina test over this C&D latest; up from 0-115 company but respected.

runners.name: Kilworth
runners.spotlight: Her worst performance in Irish points had been recorded on soft, so perhaps February's Plumpton failure on similar ground ought not to have surprised; nicely progressive in staying handicaps otherwise with wins at Taunton and Ascot, though the 7lb rise since the latter asks a stiff question.
runners.comment: Taunton and Ascot winner either side of a possibly forgivable Plumpton blip; up 7lb.

runners.name: Slim Marvel
runners.spotlight: Just one win in 24, and Ffos Las near-miss on second outing for current handler likely owed something to the drop to 0-105 company (3m, heavy); limitations exposed again since and this is tougher still.
runners.comment: Close second at Ffos Las (3m, heavy) was not built upon when back up to this class latest.

runners.name: I Spy A Diva
runners.spotlight: Sole 3m success was gained off tonight's mark in autumn 2022 (good); just one further win since (2m5f) and mixed signals this term as to her present optimum trip, but unarguably weighted to strike again.
runners.comment: Mixed signals this term as to her present optimum trip, but weighted to strike again.

races.race_name: Mercedes-Benz Of Chichester Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lisnamurrican
runners.spotlight: Won a soft-ground Irish point in December 2024; encouraging second on Huntingdon hurdle debut (2m4f, soft) a year later; a little disappointing when fifth of 16 at Warwick (2m3f, good to soft) in January but given a bit of time since and may confirm that hurdle debut promise at the second time of asking.
runners.comment: Point winner; promising second on hurdle debut; only fifth since but can bounce back

runners.name: Strong Run
runners.spotlight: Did well in bumpers and came good at the second attempt over hurdles at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft) in December; bettered that form when second at Wincanton (2m5f, heavy) in January; unable to make an impact in the EBF mares' final (handicap) at Newbury last month but bold show looks assured back in a novice.
runners.comment: Unable to make an impact in EBF final latest but should have a big say back in a novice

runners.name: Grey Gamble
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit when midfield in a couple of bumpers but not enough to suggest she's likely to make a winning start over hurdles after five months off.
runners.comment: Only minor promise in bumpers ahead of hurdle debut after five months off

runners.name: That Is Blue
runners.spotlight: Third in a Wincanton bumper (1m7f, heavy) and Plumpton maiden hurdle (2m4f, good) just under ten weeks apart; has ability but will need to raise her game to seriously trouble Lisnamurrican and Strong Run.
runners.comment: Third in a bumper and maiden hurdle but more will be needed here

runners.name: Pink Perjury
runners.spotlight: Always behind in two bumpers and likely booked for another struggle now hurdling.
runners.comment: Always behind in two bumpers ahead of switch to hurdles

runners.name: Madmoiselle Moss
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in two bumpers ahead of this switch to hurdles.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two bumpers ahead of switch to hurdles

runners.name: Precious Jean
runners.spotlight: No sign of ability over hurdles, including a low-grade handicap; a wind operation since last seen (also tongue-tied first time) needs to spark huge improvement.
runners.comment: No form over hurdles; wind op (also tongue-tied) needs to spark huge improvement

races.race_name: KSR Lighting, The Ultimate Lighting Solutions Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sweet Nightingale
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough over fences with 10l win at Exeter (3m, good) last month; was beaten by 10l when third of four at Plumpton (3m2f) last time but that was behind two progressive 6yos and her jockey went for home a circuit too soon; still has potential as a staying chaser and she's in the mix off same mark as last time.
runners.comment: Won at Exeter on penultimate run and ran better than the bare facts last time; in the mix

runners.name: Carrigmoorna Rowan
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing for Harry Fry; competitive form over hurdles and fences for current yard but he bled three runs back and couldn't hang in there at Exeter (2m3f, good) last time; continues to drop down the weights but he's now 0-8 over fences and is untried at this trip.
runners.comment: Some decent efforts for current yard but he's now 0-8 over fences and this is a new trip

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has been resurgent with wins at Hereford (3m1f, good) in his last two starts; 3lb higher than last time but he stayed on strongly to beat a clear second and remains well handicapped on his best form; key player.
runners.comment: Resurgent 8yo who has won at Hereford in his last two starts and he's a major player again

runners.name: Ballynaheer
runners.spotlight: Progressive 7yo who has form figures of 1221141 over fences since September; only two completed when he scored at Market Rasen (3m, good) 19 days ago but he still looks on a workable mark and his first chase win was over C&D; strongly respected.
runners.comment: Progressive 7yo who has form figures of 1221141 since September; strongly respected

runners.name: Gold Clermont
runners.spotlight: All four wins have came over fences Andy Irvine; her last success was in January 2024 but that was at this track (3m3f) and she returned to form with a creditable third over hurdles here (3m2f, soft) last month; that was only her second start since rejoining her former stable and she's in the mix back over fences.
runners.comment: Back to form with a third over hurdles here last month; in the mix back over fences

races.race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Calvino
runners.spotlight: Fast-ground bumper winner who landed a Taunton novice (2m3f, good) in January but he was disappointing on his return visit the following month; bit to prove after that but he's still lightly raced and it wouldn't be a surprise if he resumed his progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time but he's still unexposed and is in the mix on handicap debut

runners.name: Yellow Star
runners.spotlight: Bounced back with a win at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) in December but he was tailed off in next two starts and he didn't get very far at Newbury (fell at third) last time; has plenty to prove again and he's still 3lb higher than for his last win.
runners.comment: Bounced back with a win at Warwick in December but has form figures of 00F since then
runners.owner: Tim Lord Kc And G L Moore

runners.name: Precious Metal
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; pulled up in a Sandown handicap (2m4f, soft) last month but that was a tough assignment in a valuable event and he still has potential after only five runs in this sphere; needs checking in market on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: Had tough assignment at Sandown latest and he's still unexposed; one to keep an eye on

runners.name: Valadon
runners.spotlight: Scored with a tongue-tie added at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) last month and he ran to a similar level when a 2l third at Wincanton (2m4f, good) last time; has been as good as ever in those last two runs and he won this race last year; respected.
runners.comment: In good form in his last two runs and he won this race last year; respected

runners.name: Jackomy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who justified favouritism in a Wincanton handicap (2m4f, good) last month and followed up with a dominant display at Ludlow (2m5f, good); up another 10lb but his form has really taken off recently and he's open to more progress; big player.
runners.comment: 6yo who has won in good style in last two runs and he's open to more progress; big player

runners.name: He's A Latchico
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who has form figures of 11631 at Fontwell; has mixed record this season but he went close at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) on his penultimate run and was rallying in a close fifth when he unseated two out at Kempton (2m, good) last time; one of two runners for the yard and he's an interesting contender back at this track.
runners.comment: Went close at Plumpton on penultimate run and he's 3-5 at Fontwell; interesting contender

races.race_name: V2 Radio Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: West Orchard
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over fences including here (about 2m 3f, good) in 2024; last ran over hurdles when second at Wincanton (about 3m, good to soft) and he returns to this sphere after a couple of errors at Plumpton last month; respected.
runners.comment: Unlike most of these he's been in fair form and return to hurdles no bother; big player

runners.name: Amalfi Skyline
runners.spotlight: Hereford winner over fences (2m5f, good to soft) in 2024 who was also second over hurdles at Plumpton (3m1f, good) that year; missed 2025 and fell on his stable debut in February but there were signs he retained ability despite finishing last of five here (about 2m5f, soft) last month; possibilities in a thin event.
runners.comment: Missed 2025 but signs he retained ability at this track last time; could be the answer

runners.name: Has Troke
runners.spotlight: Course novice winner in 2022 who doubled his tally over hurdles at Lingfield (2m7f, also soft) last February; creditable seasonal reappearance in December wasn't built on and he finished 30l adrift having sprawled two out at Market Rasen two months ago; maybe just his second visit to this track will relight the fire but probably doesn't want the ground to dry out appreciably.
runners.comment: 1-1 here; creditable effort in December wasn't built on but this is not a strong event

runners.name: Fravanco
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner on the Flat who was 61l adrift when beating one home on his handicap hurdle debut at Kempton (2m) last month; just a fair effort on the Flat a fortnight ago; upped markedly in trip for his second go in this sphere.
runners.comment: Beaten 61l on handicap debut last month; up markedly in trip for this second go

runners.name: Simon The Seagull
runners.spotlight: Matched his previous best when beaten 17l in fourth at Lingfield (about 2m3f, good to soft) in November but pulled up then last (first-time visor) in two subsequent outings; cheekpieces now given a whirl and might come on for latest outing; 6lb 'wrong', though.
runners.comment: Pulled up on penultimate start and last on return; cheekpieces go on; out of the weights
runners.owner: G Grissell, Mrs A Lynch & P Edwards

runners.name: Shesupincourt
runners.spotlight: 2-31 over hurdles (soft/heavy) but the losing run is mounting up, as are a string of big defeats; the latest of those came at Hereford just eight days ago and she's out of the weights again.
runners.comment: Losing run and big defeats are racking up; out of the weights

runners.name: Approaching Storm
runners.spotlight: Not too much encouragement in three maiden hurdles at up to 2m4f but there was a whiff of something on penultimate start and this new trip might help on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Not much to shout about in three qualifying runs; new trip for h'cap debut; may do better

runners.name: Miss Jaffa Cake
runners.spotlight: Not the biggest and her form figures are uninspiring but there were hints of ability before unseating in Wincanton novice in January; subsequent efforts suggest that might have been a false dawn and she's out of the weights again.
runners.comment: Hint of ability hasn't been confirmed; out of the weights

runners.name: Counteract
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner in the spring of 2021 but he'd lost his form when last seen in November 2023; impossible to predict what this stable debut will produce but he's on a dangerous mark and the market should give firm indications.
runners.comment: Big absence but this veteran returns on a dangerous mark; watch the market on stable debut

races.race_name: A.W. Jeffreys (Southampton) Ltd Handicap Chase

runners.name: A Little Something
runners.spotlight: Had heavy ground as an excuse when pulled up on chase debut in February after nine months off and fared a lot better back on a sounder surface when second of six at Ludlow (2m4f, good) 25 days ago; her jumping was a bit iffy but is entitled to get better with experience; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Pulled up on chase debut but much better when second at Ludlow since; enters calculations

runners.name: Kitsilano
runners.spotlight: Both wins on heavy, latterly a 2m chase at Leicester in February; creditable third at Plumpton next time but didn't jump well when remote fourth over 2m5f here (good to soft) four weeks ago; on the back of that the cheekpieces he's worn on his last five outings are replaced by a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Won a 2m chase on heavy in February but remote fourth latest; new headgear on now

runners.name: Gorcombe Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Just 1-34 under rules but the win came here (in 2023) and he went very close to a second course success 16 days ago, going down by only a head over this trip on good to soft; another who could be involved in the finish.
runners.comment: 1-34 but the win came here and he also went close over C&D latest; another to consider

runners.name: Hatos
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on heavy ground; C&D second in January was also on soft but his two placed efforts over 2m4f at Wincanton since came on good to soft and good; ought to be thereabouts again but it is now over two years since his last win.
runners.comment: Placed on last three starts (including C&D) but is now over two years since last win

runners.name: Majestic Moment
runners.spotlight: His 2m2f course success in October was very fortunate (looked held in dispute of third when left to finish alone at the last) but there was no luck involved when returning from a break to land a second chase win at Exeter (2m3f, good) 17 days ago; a 4lb rise shouldn't prevent another bold bid.
runners.comment: Fortunate winner here in autumn but no luck involved in recent Exeter success; player

runners.name: It's A Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; did finish second in a 3m Wincanton handicap (good to soft) last month but only sixth back there since; switches to fences in a bid to get straight back on track for leading stable.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles, best effort when second at Wincanton penultimate start; chase debut

runners.name: King Of The Story
runners.spotlight: Had a very hit-and-miss record when last seen, with his six outings between July and November resulting in three wins (all 2m4f, good) and three pulled ups; hard to know what mood he'll show up in after five months off.
runners.comment: Three wins and three PUs from last six starts; hard to know what to expect back from break

runners.name: Millies Mite
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on her final two outings in 2025 but looked in much better form back from a break here 16 days ago, moving well close to the pace when unseating four out; has won the last two runnings of this race off similar marks and could be set for another bold bid.
runners.comment: Looked back in better form when unseating latest and has won last two runnings of this

runners.name: Jaffa Cake
runners.spotlight: Mostly disappointing since finishing second in a bumper last spring, weakening quickly and pulled up in both handicap hurdles in recent months; too soon to write off but switches to fences with something to prove; a first-time hood and tongue-tie combination is also added.
runners.comment: Still days but pulled up both handicap hurdles; switches to fences with headgear added

runners.name: Ballybay
runners.spotlight: This 9yo has raced seven times under rules for three different yards; completed for the first time for this one (including pulled up on chase debut) when fifth in 2m2f Plumpton handicap hurdle 19 days ago but it wasn't a performance which makes him of obvious interest.
runners.comment: Well-held fifth over hurdles on his only completed start this season

runners.name: Greenways
runners.spotlight: Successful over 2m at Uttoxeter (good) but out of form since, with recent 14.5l third at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) no big step back in the right direction.
runners.comment: Largely below par since Uttoxeter win last summer, finishing well-held third latest

runners.name: Fourtwofour
runners.spotlight: Beaten upwards of 43l in three maiden hurdles in Ireland and 19l third on his completed start in points there in the autumn; tough to argue his opening mark looks generous now switching to fences for a new yard.
runners.comment: Looks on stiff mark on hurdle/point form in Ireland now debuting over fences for new yard

races.race_name: Fontwell Park Weddings & Events Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blue Universe
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but he ran well to finished second off this mark at Huntingdon (2m3f, soft) in February; this is a deeper race but he's versatile ground-wise and has each-way claims again after a short break.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles but he was runner-up off this mark at Huntingdon last time; in the mix

runners.name: Superstylin
runners.spotlight: Three wins over fences this season and last two have been at Fontwell (2m2f/2m3f, good to soft); well-held second of four at Plumpton 19 days ago but that was behind an improver from the Nicholls yard; 10lb lower on this return to hurdling and he's an interesting contender in suitable conditions.
runners.comment: Three chase wins this season and he looks interesting off lower mark back over hurdles

runners.name: Jefe Triunfo
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Warwick (2m, good) last June but he's gone the wrong way in handicaps and was tailed off on Plumpton reappearance (2m4f, good) two weeks ago; mark continues to fall but he has something to prove.
runners.comment: Has gone the wrong way in handicaps and was tailed off on Plumpton reappearance; opposable

runners.name: Calshot Spit
runners.spotlight: Acts on fast and heavy ground; dual course winner, more recently when clinging on by a short head in December (2m2f, heavy ground); back on the same mark today but he's struggled in his last two runs and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won here in December but he's struggled in both runs since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Bushstreet Bowman
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in his two competed starts in novice events and he's struggled switched to handicaps in last two runs; still unexposed but he was 50-1 at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) last time and needs to leave that form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo but he's struggled in his completed starts and needs a transformation
runners.owner: Ben Pauling Racing & Dr Suzannah Turk

runners.name: Hiero Sport
runners.spotlight: Defied this mark at Fakenham (2m4f, good) in October 2024; only four runs since but he returned after another break with a front-running third at Huntingdon (2m5f) three weeks ago; should benefit from that run and he's not ruled out on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Back from another break with a fair third at Huntingdon three weeks ago; not ruled out

runners.name: Easter Icon
runners.spotlight: Productive spell from August 2024 to April 2025 over hurdles and on the Flat; has had some struggles in a light campaign this season but he showed signs of a revival at Stratford (2m6f, good) last month and has possibilities if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Course winner who showed signs of a revival at Stratford last month; could be dangerous

runners.name: Bread And Butter
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner (2m3f-2m5f) but his last success was 16 months ago and this veteran has been out of sorts in his last four runs; opposable.
runners.comment: His last success was 16 months ago and he's been out of sorts in his last four runs

runners.name: Chosen Bae
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Bangor (2m3f, soft) last February 2025 but he's been well held in eight runs this season and latest was at Hereford last week; out of the weights again and he can only be watched.
runners.comment: Well held in all eight runs this season and he's out of the weights again; no appeal

runners.name: Nelson Gate
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden under both codes; tailed off in last two hurdle runs but he showed some promise when runner-up in a juvenile event here (2m2f, good to soft) in November; now goes handicapping at this new trip and he needs watching in market after 111 days off; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Unexposed hurdler and he needs watching in market at this new trip on handicap debut

runners.name: My Lovely Horse
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in three of her five attempts over hurdles including over 2m on handicap debut at Plumpton (reported to have bled from the nose) last month; still early days in this sphere but she has lots to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Pulled up in three of her five starts over hurdles and has lots to prove

runners.name: Dontaskchris
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 8yo but he didn't show great over hurdles in 2024 which included a refusal to race over C&D; now goes handicapping but he has plenty to prove after almost two years off; hood added.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but he has plenty to prove after almost two years off; hood added

races.race_name: bet365 Esher Cup Handicap

runners.name: Laureate Crown
runners.spotlight: Didn't really kick on last year after winning at Ascot (7f, good to firm) on debut but he reappeared at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month having been gelded and defied a slow start to win by half a length; it was a small field but the clear runner-up finished second in a good handicap at Newmarket subsequently and this fellow may well be in the mix up 6lb.
runners.comment: Reappearance win came in small field but the form has been franked by the clear runner-up

runners.name: Look To The Stars
runners.spotlight: Won Kempton novice (7f, AW) last September on third start and followed up in warm conditions race at Newbury (1m, good to soft); failed to settle and didn't stay 1m2f in Group 3 Zetland Stakes at Newmarket last seen; wind op since and the hood could be a positive on reappearance; chance.
runners.comment: Two 2yo wins; failed to stay 1m2f when last seen; the hood could be a positive on return

runners.name: Nobody Knows
runners.spotlight: Well-backed winner at Ayr (7f, soft) last September on sole 2yo start and made it 2-2 in Wolverhampton novice (8.6f, AW) last month, again doing it cosily; has potential off his opening mark and the booking of Ryan Moore is a bonus.
runners.comment: 2-2, cosily as favourite each time; Ryan Moore booked; has potential off opening mark

runners.name: Organise
runners.spotlight: Impressive on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) last November and made it 2-2 in Yarmouth novice (1m, good) on recent reappearance; his inexperience was still evident last time and this well-bred colt could have lots more left in the tank for last year's winning yard; key player on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2-2 and could have lots more left in the tank for last year's winning yard; key player

runners.name: Capall Rasa
runners.spotlight: Improved upon first two runs (both on turf) to win at Southwell (7f, AW) last October, and followed up on handicap debut there (1m, AW); had just three rivals to beat last time and he's 12lb higher today with something to prove on turf, but is not discounted given the ease of that last win.
runners.comment: Best 2yo form came on AW but did it easily last time and he's not discounted back on turf

runners.name: Suspended Sentence
runners.spotlight: Won Ripon novice (1m, good to firm) last August on third start then followed up in four-runner nursery over this C&D (soft); creditable Listed fourth in France (1m, soft) in September but returns to a handicap off a mark 13lb higher than for his course win; further improvement needed.
runners.comment: Won small-field nursery over C&D last September but makes reappearance off 13lb higher
runners.owner: G C Myddelton, C F E Hill & Partner

runners.name: Goodwood Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) last June and kept on for fourth in Group 2 Vintage Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) next time; ran okay in quality conditions race at Newbury (1m, good to soft) in September when last seen but he needs to prove he can cut it off this lofty mark.
runners.comment: Good fourth in Group 2 at Goodwood last July but has to prove he can cut it off this mark

races.race_name: QuinnBet Highland National Handicap Chase (For The Julian Llewellen Palmer Memorial Trophy) (GBB)

runners.name: Busty Boy
runners.spotlight: Only 1-21 overall and clearly hard to win with, but he was runner-up in this 12 months ago and returns in form having been running well from 2m4f to 3m; in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this 12 months ago and returns in form; in the mix off a competitive mark

runners.name: Upfordebate
runners.spotlight: Went chasing last summer and he's won four times from 3m to 3m2f; now high in the ratings but still ran very well from the front at Carlisle last month (3m); unproven over this far and he does race up with the pace.
runners.comment: Four wins this season; unproven over this far and he does race up with the pace

runners.name: President Scottie
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner; has been expensive to follow over fences (0-9) and fair to say that others arrive here in better form.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow over fences and fair to say that others arrive here in better form

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: Not as good as he was but has turned a corner following wind surgery and came home clear at Sedgefield (3m2f, good); that race fell apart in behind but he would probably have won anyway; raised 8lb but had become very well handicapped.
runners.comment: Raised 8lb for his Sedgefield romp but had become very well handicapped

runners.name: Express Surprise
runners.spotlight: Only defeat in his last four races came in a good-quality 3m4f handicap at Windsor; jumped well and stayed on best at Chepstow last time (2m7f, soft) where the front two pulled well clear; raised 7lb but Ned Fox takes away 3lb out of that; respected.
runners.comment: Sound jumper who stays well and he's won three of his last four; progressive

runners.name: Fever Dream
runners.spotlight: If the ground is bordering on good then this 8yo could take some beating as he hosed up at Warwick last month (3m, good) on his second run after a break; the 8lb rise isn't the end of the world.
runners.comment: Hosed up at Warwick and could take some beating if the ground is bordering on good
runners.owner: Bedford & Noel & The Can't Say No

runners.name: Breeze Of Wind
runners.spotlight: Pulled up two runs ago but solid efforts in defeat on either side of that and he's an assured stayer at this trip; the blinkers are new, as were the cheekpieces when third at Kelso last month.
runners.comment: Pulled up two runs back but solid efforts in defeat on either side of that; assured stayer

runners.name: El Granjero
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who is 0-5 over fences but three times runner-up in this discipline and latest Warwick conqueror (3m5f, good to soft) was completing a hat-trick; likely to run well off an untouched mark.
runners.comment: Latest Warwick conqueror (3m5f, good to soft) was completing a hat-trick

runners.name: Fortunedefortunata
runners.spotlight: Had smart form when with Gordon Elliott before losing his way and selling for £3,000; fell midrace on stable debut but, with the cheekpieces back on, he outran odds of 100-1 to finish a fairly close third at Haydock (3m4f, good); if as good again here then he could have some say.
runners.comment: 100-1 third at Haydock and if as good again here he could have some say

runners.name: Brandt
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m4f handicap chase at Leopardstown in March of last year (yielding); this season hasn't gone so well but it wasn't too bad a run on his return to fences at Downpatrick last time (3m4f; yielding to soft) and unwise to put a line through him.
runners.comment: 3m4f chase winner last year and some sign of returning to form on his last run

runners.name: Noble Birth
runners.spotlight: 3-24 over hurdles and 1-8 as a chaser; left Gavin Cromwell after finishing tailed off in a claiming hurdle in early March (tried blinkers); needs a revival and he's got a lot of weight.
runners.comment: Last seen tailed off in an Irish claiming hurdle; needs a revival and has topweight

races.race_name: bet365 Gordon Richards Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Almeric
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-5; has gained all wins, including two Listed races, in his starts on softer than good but may yet show that he's fully effective on faster ground; drops back in class having been comfortably held in the Champion Stakes; top on the figures and he was progressive before that latest start.
runners.comment: Record is 3-5; progressive before latest start (Champion Stakes); top on the figures

runners.name: Saddadd
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in Listed race at Goodwood on last appearance but was far from disgraced, especially as he could have done with a stronger pace; progressive otherwise and landed a notable handicap at Newbury (good to firm) prior to following up over C&D (soft); remains of major interest.
runners.comment: Progressive until last outing when not disgraced; C&D winner; remains of major interest

runners.name: Devil's Advocate
runners.spotlight: Highlight last season was a very useful performance in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster, suggesting he's verging on Group class; creditable second, despite usual cheekpieces being removed, in AW Listed event at Kempton on reappearance; possibilities with the headgear back on.
runners.comment: Verging on Group class and has possibilities with usual cheekpieces reapplied

runners.name: King Of Cities
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Richard Hannon but remained with same owner; produced his best performance in most recent domestic start, namely a narrow success in Group 3 at last year's Ebor festival at York (1m1f, good to firm); may have more to offer this term; respected.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner for Richard Hannon in most recent domestic start; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Muddy Mooy
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW and she did well to finish third, in difficult circumstances, at Kempton (1m2f Listed) on reappearance; ties in with Devil's Advocate on that bare form but isn't guaranteed to find the required further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Ran well at Kempton on reappearance but isn't sure to progress further back on turf

races.race_name: Perth Racecourse Racing & Events Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Ran no race over hurdles on final run for previous yard (tried a visor) and again looked a tricky ride back over fences on debut for this one; the return of cheekpieces may help but she's very risky at the minute.
runners.comment: Beginning to look a tricky ride and she's very risky at the minute

runners.name: Roaring Conquest
runners.spotlight: A winner the last time he contested a Class 3 handicap (on reappearance) and he was boxing on when beaten a little under 10l in a deep Uttoxeter handicap last time out (2m7f, soft); looks sure to stay this far and has the assistance of Sean Bowen.
runners.comment: Positive last run; looks sure to stay this far and has the assistance of Sean Bowen

runners.name: Inferno Sacree
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; having his 40th career start but a first over 3m when successful at Taunton and he followed up at Plumpton (3m1f; again good to soft); subsequent defeat, again under Murray Dodd, suggests others in here might be better treated.
runners.comment: Front-runner; hat-trick bid came up shy and others in here might be better treated

runners.name: Jukebox Fury
runners.spotlight: Last season he won 3m handicaps at Doncaster and Wetherby (both on good ground); his Ffos Las return was encouraging given the time he'd been absent and the soft conditions; in this scenario he's unexposed; considered.
runners.comment: Ffos Las return was encouraging given the time he'd been absent and the soft conditions

runners.name: Sleedagh
runners.spotlight: Last run too bad to be true but had been consistent in novice/maidens and the previous third at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) came in a strong race; the potential is there to be competitive off this mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: The potential is there to be competitive off this mark on handicap debut

runners.name: Gwennie May Star
runners.spotlight: Won races for Dan Skelton (2m1f/2m3f) and Olly Murphy (3m1f); now on a career-high mark, off which he was no match for the front two on stable debut at Bangor (2m7f).
runners.comment: On career-high mark, off which he was no match for the front two on stable debut at Bangor

runners.name: La Zoubida
runners.spotlight: Bumper and dual hurdles winner; penalties have been finding her out so handicaps now look a better option, but with her unraced beyond 2m5f there are some stamina doubts over this far.
runners.comment: Useful novice but with her unraced beyond 2m5f there are some stamina doubts over this far

runners.name: Whistle Stop Tour
runners.spotlight: Well beaten/pulled up over fences on his last two starts but has each-way claims on the pick of his form over hurdles this season and Gregor Walkingshaw gets a tune out of him.
runners.comment: Gregor Walkingshaw gets a tune out of him and returning to hurdles looks a good thing
runners.owner: Mrs Jo Tracey, K Alexander & Russell

runners.name: Wa Wa
runners.spotlight: Had lots of racing in Ireland before joining this yard last year; returned from a wind operation to win this 12 months ago off this very mark but he's an 11yo now and his last two runs, over fences and hurdles, weren't that encouraging.
runners.comment: Won this last year off the same mark but he ran no race last time at Warwick

runners.name: Go To War
runners.spotlight: After a brief stint over fences he got back on track in beating 14 rivals over 3m at Windsor (soft) under a positive ride; however, there was a further reminder of his inconsistency when he dropped away tamely at Haydock in February; what does heighten interest is a pretty good record when fresh.
runners.comment: Poor last time and inconsistent; his pretty good record when fresh heightens interest

runners.name: Highland Fashion
runners.spotlight: His first run at about 3m when winning at Newcastle (good to soft) and then ran second at Warwick; latest Musselburgh run wasn't too shoddy given he was held up off the pace before staying on and he reportedly bled that day; had a break since and wouldn't be fully exposed as a stayer.
runners.comment: Had a break since bleeding last time and he's still unexposed as a stayer

runners.name: Jupiter Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who was very well backed at Carlisle three weeks ago and ran out a ready winner (3m1f, good to soft); raised 6lb but appeals as a horse with more to offer.
runners.comment: Justified market support last time; raised 6lb but appeals as a horse with more to offer

races.race_name: bet365 Mile (Group 2)

runners.name: Opera Ballo
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who readily bagged Group 1 honours at Meydan (1m1f, good) three months ago, taking record to 6-8; however, appears to need further improvement to defy 5lb penalty and was no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic outing.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at Meydan last time; no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic start

runners.name: Field Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Very progressive until having plausible excuses in the Sussex Stakes (found to be lame) and QEII (draw to some extent) in last two appearances; impressive wins in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and St James's Palace Stakes make him the one to beat, provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Impressive in Irish 2,000 Guineas and SJP prior to excusable defeats; sets the standard

runners.name: Never So Brave
runners.spotlight: Did very well (3-5) last season for new stable, completing a notable summer hat-trick (7f/1m, good to firm) that culminated in Group 1 success at York; not disgraced from unfavourable draw in the QEII on final outing; may rate higher still and looks likely to go well.
runners.comment: Did very well last season for new yard; not disgraced in QEII; may rate higher still

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive in his first campaign last year and still unbeaten (4-4); won going away from Opera Ballo in Group 2 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on final start; remains one to follow and has the makings of a Group 1 colt this term; commands respect.
runners.comment: Highly progressive in his first season and still unbeaten (4-4); remains one to follow
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who finished third in this race last year; returns to Sandown but may be flattered by his new rating, having been perhaps helped by the draw when registering a 100-1 success in the Group 1 QEII at Ascot (good) on last appearance; needs to prove he can back up that form.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up the form of his 100-1 win in the Group 1 QEII

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Below best in three of his last four outings in 2025 but commenced the campaign splendidly, winning twice (including in this race) then going close in the Group 1 Lockinge; while today may be a good time to catch him, he faces a slightly stiffer assignment in this field.
runners.comment: Won this race last year but is facing a slightly stiffer assignment in this field

races.race_name: bet365 Classic Trial (Group 3)

runners.name: Action
runners.spotlight: Closely related to last year's Derby winner Lambourn and has his own Epsom aspirations, having ended a progressive 2yo campaign with close second to one of his stablemates in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy); now goes in tongue-tie; the pick on the figures and may well rate higher still.
runners.comment: Ended progressive 2yo campaign with close second in the Group 1 Futurity; the form pick
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Al Zanati
runners.spotlight: Dubawi colt who got up in the final strides to win 1m1f AW Group 3 at Chantilly on final 2yo start, taking record to 2-3; looks likely to be well suited by going up further in distance; doesn't have a Derby entry but further progress may materialise.
runners.comment: AW Group 3 win in France took his record to 2-3; further progress may materialise

runners.name: Winding Stream
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, scoring comfortably at Southwell (7f) as a 2yo then running well under a penalty at Kempton (1m); holds a Derby entry and may be suited by going up further in distance today but faces a stiff task at this level; bottom on bare figures.
runners.comment: AW form comprises a win and good second; faces a stiffer task at this level

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching second in the Convivial Maiden at York then went one better at Yarmouth and ran well in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) upped further in trip; developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer.
runners.comment: Developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer

runners.name: Oceans Four
runners.spotlight: Narrowly bagged Group 3 honours at Chantilly (1m, soft) in September when last seen but still needs to prove he can repeat the clear peak form he appeared to show when going close at 25-1 in an exciting Solario at Sandown; gelding who has no major entries in the future.
runners.comment: Clear peak form when running well in the Solario; won French Group 3 next time

runners.name: Wise Prince
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt whose two major entries include the Derby; scored comfortably at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in sole 2yo start, looking the type who'll be suited by middle distances and could develop into a Group performer this year; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Nottingham, looking the type who could become a Group colt

runners.name: Raaheeb
runners.spotlight: Smartly bred, being a brother to Baaeed and Hukum; duly showed strong promise in justifying favouritism at Ascot (7f, soft) in sole 2yo outing; held a Royal Lodge entry at the time and currently holds three major entries, including the Derby; should be suited by middle distances; bright prospect.
runners.comment: Smartly bred; justified favouritism at Ascot in sole 2yo outing; bright prospect

races.race_name: bet365 Gordon Richards Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Almeric
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-5; has gained all wins, including two Listed races, in his starts on softer than good but may yet show that he's fully effective on faster ground; drops back in class having been comfortably held in the Champion Stakes; top on the figures and he was progressive before that latest start.
runners.comment: Record is 3-5; progressive before latest start (Champion Stakes); top on the figures

runners.name: Saddadd
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in Listed race at Goodwood on last appearance but was far from disgraced, especially as he could have done with a stronger pace; progressive otherwise and landed a notable handicap at Newbury (good to firm) prior to following up over C&D (soft); remains of major interest.
runners.comment: Progressive until last outing when not disgraced; C&D winner; remains of major interest

runners.name: Devil's Advocate
runners.spotlight: Highlight last season was a very useful performance in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster, suggesting he's verging on Group class; creditable second, despite usual cheekpieces being removed, in AW Listed event at Kempton on reappearance; possibilities with the headgear back on.
runners.comment: Verging on Group class and has possibilities with usual cheekpieces reapplied

runners.name: King Of Cities
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Richard Hannon but remained with same owner; produced his best performance in most recent domestic start, namely a narrow success in Group 3 at last year's Ebor festival at York (1m1f, good to firm); may have more to offer this term; respected.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner for Richard Hannon in most recent domestic start; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Muddy Mooy
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW and she did well to finish third, in difficult circumstances, at Kempton (1m2f Listed) on reappearance; ties in with Devil's Advocate on that bare form but isn't guaranteed to find the required further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Ran well at Kempton on reappearance but isn't sure to progress further back on turf

races.race_name: bet365 Mile (Group 2)

runners.name: Opera Ballo
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who readily bagged Group 1 honours at Meydan (1m1f, good) three months ago, taking record to 6-8; however, appears to need further improvement to defy 5lb penalty and was no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic outing.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at Meydan last time; no match for Zeus Olympios in last domestic start

runners.name: Field Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Very progressive until having plausible excuses in the Sussex Stakes (found to be lame) and QEII (draw to some extent) in last two appearances; impressive wins in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and St James's Palace Stakes make him the one to beat, provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Impressive in Irish 2,000 Guineas and SJP prior to excusable defeats; sets the standard

runners.name: Never So Brave
runners.spotlight: Did very well (3-5) last season for new stable, completing a notable summer hat-trick (7f/1m, good to firm) that culminated in Group 1 success at York; not disgraced from unfavourable draw in the QEII on final outing; may rate higher still and looks likely to go well.
runners.comment: Did very well last season for new yard; not disgraced in QEII; may rate higher still

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive in his first campaign last year and still unbeaten (4-4); won going away from Opera Ballo in Group 2 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on final start; remains one to follow and has the makings of a Group 1 colt this term; commands respect.
runners.comment: Highly progressive in his first season and still unbeaten (4-4); remains one to follow
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who finished third in this race last year; returns to Sandown but may be flattered by his new rating, having been perhaps helped by the draw when registering a 100-1 success in the Group 1 QEII at Ascot (good) on last appearance; needs to prove he can back up that form.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up the form of his 100-1 win in the Group 1 QEII

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Below best in three of his last four outings in 2025 but commenced the campaign splendidly, winning twice (including in this race) then going close in the Group 1 Lockinge; while today may be a good time to catch him, he faces a slightly stiffer assignment in this field.
runners.comment: Won this race last year but is facing a slightly stiffer assignment in this field

races.race_name: bet365 Classic Trial (Group 3)

runners.name: Action
runners.spotlight: Closely related to last year's Derby winner Lambourn and has his own Epsom aspirations, having ended a progressive 2yo campaign with close second to one of his stablemates in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy); now goes in tongue-tie; the pick on the figures and may well rate higher still.
runners.comment: Ended progressive 2yo campaign with close second in the Group 1 Futurity; the form pick
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Al Zanati
runners.spotlight: Dubawi colt who got up in the final strides to win 1m1f AW Group 3 at Chantilly on final 2yo start, taking record to 2-3; looks likely to be well suited by going up further in distance; doesn't have a Derby entry but further progress may materialise.
runners.comment: AW Group 3 win in France took his record to 2-3; further progress may materialise

runners.name: Winding Stream
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, scoring comfortably at Southwell (7f) as a 2yo then running well under a penalty at Kempton (1m); holds a Derby entry and may be suited by going up further in distance today but faces a stiff task at this level; bottom on bare figures.
runners.comment: AW form comprises a win and good second; faces a stiffer task at this level

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching second in the Convivial Maiden at York then went one better at Yarmouth and ran well in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) upped further in trip; developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer.
runners.comment: Developing into a useful Flat performer for his esteemed dual-purpose trainer

runners.name: Oceans Four
runners.spotlight: Narrowly bagged Group 3 honours at Chantilly (1m, soft) in September when last seen but still needs to prove he can repeat the clear peak form he appeared to show when going close at 25-1 in an exciting Solario at Sandown; gelding who has no major entries in the future.
runners.comment: Clear peak form when running well in the Solario; won French Group 3 next time

runners.name: Wise Prince
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt whose two major entries include the Derby; scored comfortably at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in sole 2yo start, looking the type who'll be suited by middle distances and could develop into a Group performer this year; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Nottingham, looking the type who could become a Group colt

runners.name: Raaheeb
runners.spotlight: Smartly bred, being a brother to Baaeed and Hukum; duly showed strong promise in justifying favouritism at Ascot (7f, soft) in sole 2yo outing; held a Royal Lodge entry at the time and currently holds three major entries, including the Derby; should be suited by middle distances; bright prospect.
runners.comment: Smartly bred; justified favouritism at Ascot in sole 2yo outing; bright prospect
